---
title: "p28409 - CTCL CNV Trees (Published Pipeline: NK Reference, Pooled Blood/Skin)"
output:
  html_document:
    self_contained: true
    includes:
      in_header: !expr system.file("templates/fgcz_header.html", package = "ezRun", lib.loc = .libPaths())
    css: !expr system.file("templates/fgcz.css", package = "ezRun", lib.loc = .libPaths())
    code_folding: hide
editor_options:
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE, comment = "")
```

CNV trees rebuilt from the **published inferCNV runs** (Geier et al. 2024 paper code, `06_Inferred_copy_number_variations_in_scRNAseq.R`). Two differences vs the 2023 per-patient runs used in `01b`:

| | 2023 FGCZ runs (used in 01b) | Published runs (this report) |
|---|---|---|
| Reference | CD8+ T (MF) / **CD4+ T contaminated** (SS) | **NK cells** (`refgrp-1` + `refgrp-2`) |
| Granularity | Per-patient | Pooled **blood** / pooled **skin** |
| Observation cells | Per-patient CD4 (MF), CD8+Malign.T (SS) | All T-cell subsets including **per-patient malignant groups** (`Malignant T1` … `T8`) |
| Subclustering | density (default) | `leiden`, `BayesMaxPNormal=0.2`, `num_ref_groups=2`, `sd_amplifier=3` |

**Why this matters:** in the 2023 SS run the `CD4+ T` reference contained mTCM cells, so mTCM was invisible as a subclone. With NK as reference, mTCM stays in the observations — subclones now span both mTCM and mTRM. Andrea ran two pooled jobs: `240227_skin_T_NK_ref/` (full output, ~1,500 obs cells) and `240523_blood_T_NK16_ref/` (`run.final.infercnv_obj` only, ~41k obs cells).

# {.tabset}

## Reference UMAP

```{r load-mal, include=FALSE}
library(Seurat)
library(qs2)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
library(dplyr)

seurat_path <- "/srv/GT/analysis/p28409-Andrea/switchdrive_data/CTCL_blood_skin_malignant.qs2"
mal_obj <- qs_read(seurat_path, nthreads = 16)

mal_colors    <- c("mTCM" = "darkgreen", "mTRM" = "darkred", "mCyto" = "darkblue")  # paper palette (09_Transcriptional_heterogeneity.R)
tissue_colors <- c("blood" = "#3C5488", "skin" = "#E64B35")

# barcode → meta lookup
mal_meta <- mal_obj@meta.data |>
  dplyr::mutate(barcode = colnames(mal_obj)) |>
  dplyr::select(barcode, Malignant_T, tissue, patient)
```

`r format(ncol(mal_obj), big.mark = ",")` malignant T cells (mTCM/mTRM/mCyto only) from blood + skin, used as the **reference Seurat object** for barcode matching, Malignant_T overlays, and subclone composition.

### Malignant_T state

```{r umap-state, fig.width=8, fig.height=7}
DimPlot(mal_obj, group.by = "Malignant_T", cols = mal_colors,
        raster = FALSE, order = TRUE, repel = TRUE) +
  theme(aspect.ratio = 1) + ggtitle("Malignant_T state")
```

### Tissue + Patient

```{r umap-tissue-pat, fig.width=18, fig.height=7}
p_t <- DimPlot(mal_obj, group.by = "tissue", cols = tissue_colors,
               raster = FALSE, order = TRUE) +
  theme(aspect.ratio = 1) + ggtitle("Tissue")
p_p <- DimPlot(mal_obj, group.by = "patient", raster = FALSE,
               cols = unname(pals::polychrome()), order = TRUE, repel = TRUE) +
  theme(aspect.ratio = 1) + ggtitle("Patient")
p_t | p_p
```

## inferCNV Setup

```{r load-infercnv, include=FALSE}
cache_setup <- "/srv/GT/analysis/p28409-Andrea/2026-03-24-UPhyloplot2-CNV-Trees/cache_01c_infercnv_setup.qs2"

if (file.exists(cache_setup)) {
  setup <- qs_read(cache_setup, nthreads = 8)
} else {
  skin_obj  <- readRDS("/srv/GT/analysis/p28409-Andrea/InferCNV_published_NK_ref/skin/run.final.infercnv_obj")
  blood_obj <- readRDS("/srv/GT/analysis/p28409-Andrea/InferCNV_published_NK_ref/blood/run.final.infercnv_obj")

  group_counts <- function(o, tissue) {
    ref <- sapply(o@reference_grouped_cell_indices, length)
    obs <- sapply(o@observation_grouped_cell_indices, length)
    rbind(
      data.frame(tissue = tissue, role = "reference",   cell_type = names(ref), n = unname(ref)),
      data.frame(tissue = tissue, role = "observation", cell_type = names(obs), n = unname(obs))
    )
  }
  setup_tbl <- rbind(group_counts(skin_obj, "skin"), group_counts(blood_obj, "blood"))

  # per-malignant-group subcluster sizes for tree planning
  collect_subc <- function(o, tissue) {
    sc <- o@tumor_subclusters$subclusters
    ct_mal <- grep("^Malignant", names(sc), value = TRUE)
    do.call(rbind, lapply(ct_mal, function(ct) {
      sizes <- sapply(sc[[ct]], length)
      data.frame(
        tissue = tissue, cell_type = ct, n_subclones = length(sizes),
        n_cells = sum(sizes),
        size_top5 = paste(head(sort(sizes, decreasing = TRUE), 5), collapse = "/")
      )
    }))
  }
  subc_tbl <- rbind(collect_subc(skin_obj, "skin"), collect_subc(blood_obj, "blood"))

  setup <- list(setup_tbl = setup_tbl, subc_tbl = subc_tbl,
                skin_n_cells = ncol(skin_obj@expr.data), skin_n_genes = nrow(skin_obj@expr.data),
                blood_n_cells = ncol(blood_obj@expr.data), blood_n_genes = nrow(blood_obj@expr.data))
  qs_save(setup, cache_setup, nthreads = 8)
  rm(skin_obj, blood_obj); gc(verbose = FALSE)
}
```

**Object sizes:**

- Skin: `r format(setup$skin_n_cells, big.mark = ",")` cells × `r format(setup$skin_n_genes, big.mark = ",")` genes
- Blood: `r format(setup$blood_n_cells, big.mark = ",")` cells × `r format(setup$blood_n_genes, big.mark = ",")` genes

### Cell groups used by inferCNV

```{r show-setup}
setup$setup_tbl |>
  dplyr::arrange(tissue, role, -n) |>
  knitr::kable(caption = "Cells per group (reference vs observation) in each inferCNV run")
```

### Per-patient malignant subclones (leiden)

`Malignant Tx` group names correspond to patient X (blood and skin numbering is independent). Top-5 subclone sizes shown.

```{r show-subclones}
setup$subc_tbl |>
  dplyr::arrange(tissue, cell_type) |>
  knitr::kable(caption = "Leiden subclone structure within each malignant group")
```

## Barcode Matching

```{r match-bc, include=FALSE}
cache_match <- "/srv/GT/analysis/p28409-Andrea/2026-03-24-UPhyloplot2-CNV-Trees/cache_01c_match.qs2"

if (file.exists(cache_match)) {
  match_data <- qs_read(cache_match, nthreads = 8)
} else {
  skin_obj  <- readRDS("/srv/GT/analysis/p28409-Andrea/InferCNV_published_NK_ref/skin/run.final.infercnv_obj")
  blood_obj <- readRDS("/srv/GT/analysis/p28409-Andrea/InferCNV_published_NK_ref/blood/run.final.infercnv_obj")

  build_long <- function(o, tissue) {
    sc_list <- o@tumor_subclusters$subclusters
    ct_mal <- grep("^Malignant", names(sc_list), value = TRUE)
    long <- do.call(rbind, lapply(ct_mal, function(ct) {
      sc <- sc_list[[ct]]
      do.call(rbind, lapply(names(sc), function(sid) {
        idx <- sc[[sid]]
        data.frame(cell_type = ct, subclone_id = sid,
                   barcode = colnames(o@expr.data)[idx],
                   stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
      }))
    }))
    long$tissue <- tissue
    long
  }
  long_skin  <- build_long(skin_obj, "skin")
  long_blood <- build_long(blood_obj, "blood")
  long_all <- rbind(long_skin, long_blood)
  long_all$patient_id_inferCNV <- sub("^Malignant ", "", long_all$cell_type)

  # Rename Seurat-side tissue → tissue_seurat to avoid collision; inferCNV-side tissue is authoritative
  mal_meta_join <- mal_meta |> dplyr::rename(tissue_seurat = tissue)

  matched <- long_all |>
    dplyr::left_join(mal_meta_join, by = "barcode") |>
    dplyr::mutate(matched = !is.na(Malignant_T))

  match_summary <- matched |>
    dplyr::group_by(tissue, cell_type) |>
    dplyr::summarise(
      n_inferCNV = dplyr::n(),
      n_matched_Seurat = sum(matched),
      pct_matched = round(100 * n_matched_Seurat / n_inferCNV, 1),
      n_mTCM = sum(matched & Malignant_T == "mTCM", na.rm = TRUE),
      n_mTRM = sum(matched & Malignant_T == "mTRM", na.rm = TRUE),
      n_mCyto = sum(matched & Malignant_T == "mCyto", na.rm = TRUE),
      patients_seurat = paste(sort(unique(stats::na.omit(patient))), collapse = ", "),
      .groups = "drop"
    )

  match_data <- list(long = matched, summary = match_summary)
  qs_save(match_data, cache_match, nthreads = 8)
  rm(skin_obj, blood_obj); gc(verbose = FALSE)
}
```

For each `Malignant Tx` group from inferCNV, count cells that also exist in the malignant Seurat object (mTCM/mTRM/mCyto). Unmatched cells are mostly QC-filtered cells removed downstream of the inferCNV run.

```{r show-match}
match_data$summary |>
  knitr::kable(caption = "Match rate of inferCNV malignant groups → Seurat malignant object")
```

```{r match-by-state, fig.width=10, fig.height=5}
match_data$long |>
  dplyr::filter(matched) |>
  dplyr::count(tissue, cell_type, Malignant_T) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = cell_type, y = n, fill = Malignant_T)) +
  geom_col(position = "fill") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = mal_colors) +
  facet_wrap(~ tissue, scales = "free_x") +
  theme_bw() + theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(x = "inferCNV malignant group", y = "Fraction (matched cells)",
       title = "Composition of each inferCNV malignant group (Seurat Malignant_T labels)")
```

## CNV Signal in UMAP {.tabset}

```{r cnv-umap-prep, include=FALSE}
# For each patient, project inferCNV cells onto the Seurat UMAP
umap_df <- as.data.frame(mal_obj@reductions$umap@cell.embeddings)
umap_df$barcode <- rownames(umap_df)
umap_df <- umap_df |> dplyr::left_join(mal_meta, by = "barcode")
colnames(umap_df)[1:2] <- c("UMAP_1", "UMAP_2")

# subclone assignments per cell
sc_assign <- match_data$long |>
  dplyr::filter(matched) |>
  dplyr::select(barcode, tissue_infercnv = tissue, cell_type, subclone_id, patient_id_inferCNV)

# pick patients to display: those with >=50 matched cells in inferCNV
top_patients <- match_data$summary |>
  dplyr::filter(n_matched_Seurat >= 30) |>
  dplyr::arrange(tissue, dplyr::desc(n_matched_Seurat))
```

For each patient (with ≥30 matched cells), show the inferCNV cells overlaid on the malignant UMAP, coloured by (1) Malignant_T state and (2) leiden subclone. Patients where mTCM **and** mTRM coexist in subclones confirm that the NK-reference run does not bias toward mTRM.

```{r cnv-umap-tabs, results='asis', fig.width=16, fig.height=7}
plot_patient_umap <- function(pat_id, tissue_in) {
  pat_meta <- match_data$long |>
    dplyr::filter(matched, patient_id_inferCNV == pat_id, tissue == tissue_in) |>
    dplyr::distinct(barcode, .keep_all = TRUE)
  if (nrow(pat_meta) == 0) return(NULL)
  pat_xy <- umap_df |> dplyr::inner_join(
    pat_meta |> dplyr::select(barcode, subclone_id, cell_type), by = "barcode"
  )
  # aggregate tiny subclones for legibility
  sc_counts <- pat_xy |> dplyr::count(subclone_id) |> dplyr::arrange(-n)
  big <- sc_counts$subclone_id[sc_counts$n >= max(5, ceiling(nrow(pat_xy) * 0.02))]
  pat_xy$subclone_plot <- ifelse(pat_xy$subclone_id %in% big, pat_xy$subclone_id, "minor")
  bg <- umap_df

  p1 <- ggplot(bg, aes(UMAP_1, UMAP_2)) +
    geom_point(color = "grey85", size = 0.2, alpha = 0.5) +
    geom_point(data = pat_xy, aes(color = Malignant_T), size = 1) +
    scale_color_manual(values = mal_colors) +
    theme_bw() + theme(aspect.ratio = 1) +
    ggtitle(sprintf("%s — %s (n=%d) — Malignant_T", tissue_in, pat_id, nrow(pat_xy)))

  n_sub <- length(unique(pat_xy$subclone_plot))
  sub_pal <- if (n_sub <= 24) unname(pals::tableau20())[1:max(2, n_sub)] else unname(pals::polychrome())[1:n_sub]
  names(sub_pal) <- unique(pat_xy$subclone_plot)
  if ("minor" %in% names(sub_pal)) sub_pal["minor"] <- "grey60"

  p2 <- ggplot(bg, aes(UMAP_1, UMAP_2)) +
    geom_point(color = "grey85", size = 0.2, alpha = 0.5) +
    geom_point(data = pat_xy, aes(color = subclone_plot), size = 1) +
    scale_color_manual(values = sub_pal) +
    theme_bw() + theme(aspect.ratio = 1) +
    ggtitle(sprintf("%s — %s — leiden subclones (≥2%%)", tissue_in, pat_id))
  print(p1 | p2)
  cat("\n\n")
}

for (i in seq_len(nrow(top_patients))) {
  pat <- top_patients$cell_type[i] |> sub("^Malignant ", "", x = _)
  tis <- top_patients$tissue[i]
  cat(sprintf("\n### %s — %s\n\n", tis, top_patients$cell_type[i]))
  plot_patient_umap(pat, tis)
}
```

## Subclone Composition

For each major leiden subclone (≥3% of its malignant group), show the fraction of mTCM / mTRM / mCyto cells. With NK reference, mTCM is now properly represented in subclones across all patients.

```{r subclone-comp, fig.width=14, fig.height=10}
sc_comp <- match_data$long |>
  dplyr::filter(matched) |>
  dplyr::group_by(tissue, cell_type, subclone_id) |>
  dplyr::summarise(n_total = dplyr::n(),
                   n_mTCM = sum(Malignant_T == "mTCM"),
                   n_mTRM = sum(Malignant_T == "mTRM"),
                   n_mCyto = sum(Malignant_T == "mCyto"),
                   .groups = "drop") |>
  dplyr::group_by(tissue, cell_type) |>
  dplyr::mutate(pct_of_patient = 100 * n_total / sum(n_total)) |>
  dplyr::ungroup() |>
  dplyr::filter(pct_of_patient >= 3)

sc_long <- sc_comp |>
  dplyr::select(tissue, cell_type, subclone_id, mTCM = n_mTCM, mTRM = n_mTRM, mCyto = n_mCyto) |>
  tidyr::pivot_longer(c(mTCM, mTRM, mCyto), names_to = "state", values_to = "n")

ggplot(sc_long, aes(x = subclone_id, y = n, fill = state)) +
  geom_col(position = "fill") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = mal_colors) +
  facet_wrap(~ tissue + cell_type, scales = "free_x", ncol = 3) +
  theme_bw() + theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 60, hjust = 1, size = 7)) +
  labs(x = "Leiden subclone", y = "Fraction of cells",
       title = "Malignant_T composition per subclone (subclones ≥3% of patient)")
```

## CNV Heatmaps {.tabset}

```{r cnv-heatmaps-prep, include=FALSE}
cache_hm <- "/srv/GT/analysis/p28409-Andrea/2026-03-24-UPhyloplot2-CNV-Trees/cache_01c_heatmaps.qs2"

build_hm_mat <- function(inf_obj_path, malignant_pat_label, max_cells_per_subclone = 50) {
  o <- readRDS(inf_obj_path)
  sc_list <- o@tumor_subclusters$subclusters[[malignant_pat_label]]
  if (is.null(sc_list)) return(NULL)
  # sample cells from each subclone for tractability
  set.seed(42)
  sampled <- lapply(names(sc_list), function(sid) {
    idx <- sc_list[[sid]]
    if (length(idx) > max_cells_per_subclone) idx <- sample(idx, max_cells_per_subclone)
    data.frame(subclone_id = sid, idx = idx)
  })
  picked <- do.call(rbind, sampled)
  # filter subclones with <5 cells in the sample set
  keep_sc <- picked |> dplyr::count(subclone_id) |> dplyr::filter(n >= 5) |> dplyr::pull(subclone_id)
  picked <- picked[picked$subclone_id %in% keep_sc, ]
  mat <- o@expr.data[, picked$idx, drop = FALSE]
  list(mat = mat, picked = picked, gene_order = o@gene_order,
       barcodes = colnames(o@expr.data)[picked$idx])
}

if (file.exists(cache_hm)) {
  hm_data <- qs_read(cache_hm, nthreads = 8)
} else {
  skin_path  <- "/srv/GT/analysis/p28409-Andrea/InferCNV_published_NK_ref/skin/run.final.infercnv_obj"
  blood_path <- "/srv/GT/analysis/p28409-Andrea/InferCNV_published_NK_ref/blood/run.final.infercnv_obj"

  hm_data <- list()
  for (tis in c("skin", "blood")) {
    pats <- top_patients$cell_type[top_patients$tissue == tis]
    for (p in pats) {
      key <- paste(tis, p, sep = "::")
      msg <- sprintf("Building heatmap matrix: %s\n", key); cat(msg)
      path <- if (tis == "skin") skin_path else blood_path
      hm_data[[key]] <- build_hm_mat(path, p, max_cells_per_subclone = 40)
    }
  }
  qs_save(hm_data, cache_hm, nthreads = 8)
}
```

Per patient: CNV signal heatmap (rows = genes ordered by chromosome, columns = cells grouped by leiden subclone). For tractability, up to 40 cells are sampled per subclone. Subclones with <5 cells after sampling are dropped. Cells colored by leiden subclone (top annotation) and Malignant_T state.

```{r cnv-heatmaps, results='asis', fig.width=14, fig.height=8}
suppressPackageStartupMessages({library(ComplexHeatmap); library(circlize)})

plot_one_heatmap <- function(key, hm_data, match_long) {
  d <- hm_data[[key]]
  if (is.null(d)) return(invisible(NULL))
  mat <- d$mat
  bc  <- d$barcodes
  picked <- d$picked
  # build cell annotations
  ca <- match_long |>
    dplyr::filter(barcode %in% bc) |>
    dplyr::distinct(barcode, .keep_all = TRUE)
  cell_order <- order(picked$subclone_id)
  mat <- mat[, cell_order, drop = FALSE]
  picked <- picked[cell_order, ]
  bc <- bc[cell_order]
  ca <- ca[match(bc, ca$barcode), ]

  # gene chromosomal order
  go <- d$gene_order
  go <- go[rownames(mat), , drop = FALSE]
  go_order <- order(go$chr, go$start)
  mat <- mat[go_order, , drop = FALSE]
  go <- go[go_order, , drop = FALSE]

  # subclone palette
  sub_ids <- unique(picked$subclone_id)
  sub_pal <- if (length(sub_ids) <= 24) unname(pals::tableau20())[seq_along(sub_ids)] else unname(pals::polychrome())[seq_along(sub_ids)]
  names(sub_pal) <- sub_ids

  top_ann <- HeatmapAnnotation(
    subclone = picked$subclone_id,
    Malignant_T = ifelse(is.na(ca$Malignant_T), "unmatched", ca$Malignant_T),
    col = list(
      subclone = sub_pal,
      Malignant_T = c(mal_colors, unmatched = "grey80")
    ),
    show_annotation_name = TRUE, simple_anno_size = unit(3, "mm")
  )
  # chromosome row annotation
  chr_levels <- unique(go$chr)
  chr_colors <- setNames(rep(c("#444444", "#BBBBBB"), length.out = length(chr_levels)), chr_levels)
  row_ann <- rowAnnotation(chr = anno_simple(as.character(go$chr), col = chr_colors,
                                              border = FALSE),
                            show_annotation_name = FALSE)

  rng <- range(mat)
  col_fun <- circlize::colorRamp2(c(rng[1], 1, rng[2]), c("#00428c", "white", "#9e002a"))

  ht <- Heatmap(t(mat),
    name = "CNV",
    col = col_fun,
    cluster_rows = FALSE, cluster_columns = FALSE,
    show_row_names = FALSE, show_column_names = FALSE,
    top_annotation = NULL,
    left_annotation = rowAnnotation(
      subclone = picked$subclone_id,
      Malignant_T = ifelse(is.na(ca$Malignant_T), "unmatched", ca$Malignant_T),
      col = list(subclone = sub_pal, Malignant_T = c(mal_colors, unmatched = "grey80")),
      simple_anno_size = unit(3, "mm")
    ),
    column_split = factor(go$chr, levels = chr_levels),
    column_title_gp = gpar(fontsize = 8),
    column_gap = unit(0.5, "mm"),
    border = TRUE,
    use_raster = TRUE,
    row_title = NULL,
    column_title = sprintf("%s — CNV signal (rows=cells grouped by subclone, cols=genes by chr)", key)
  )
  draw(ht)
}

for (key in names(hm_data)) {
  parts <- strsplit(key, "::")[[1]]
  cat(sprintf("\n### %s — %s\n\n", parts[1], parts[2]))
  plot_one_heatmap(key, hm_data, match_data$long)
  cat("\n\n")
}
```

## Subclone Phylogenetic Trees {.tabset}

```{r trees-prep, include=FALSE}
cache_trees <- "/srv/GT/analysis/p28409-Andrea/2026-03-24-UPhyloplot2-CNV-Trees/cache_01c_trees.qs2"

# compute subclone centroids in CNV space and build hierarchical tree
build_centroid_tree <- function(inf_obj_path, malignant_pat_label, min_sc_size = 5) {
  o <- readRDS(inf_obj_path)
  sc_list <- o@tumor_subclusters$subclusters[[malignant_pat_label]]
  if (is.null(sc_list)) return(NULL)
  sc_sizes <- sapply(sc_list, length)
  keep <- names(sc_sizes)[sc_sizes >= min_sc_size]
  if (length(keep) < 2) return(NULL)
  centroids <- sapply(keep, function(sid) {
    idx <- sc_list[[sid]]
    rowMeans(o@expr.data[, idx, drop = FALSE])
  })
  d <- dist(t(centroids), method = "euclidean")
  hc <- hclust(d, method = "ward.D2")
  list(hc = hc, sizes = sc_sizes[keep],
       barcodes_per_sc = lapply(keep, function(sid) colnames(o@expr.data)[sc_list[[sid]]]))
}

if (file.exists(cache_trees)) {
  trees <- qs_read(cache_trees, nthreads = 8)
} else {
  skin_path  <- "/srv/GT/analysis/p28409-Andrea/InferCNV_published_NK_ref/skin/run.final.infercnv_obj"
  blood_path <- "/srv/GT/analysis/p28409-Andrea/InferCNV_published_NK_ref/blood/run.final.infercnv_obj"
  trees <- list()
  for (tis in c("skin", "blood")) {
    pats <- top_patients$cell_type[top_patients$tissue == tis]
    for (p in pats) {
      key <- paste(tis, p, sep = "::")
      cat(sprintf("Building tree: %s\n", key))
      path <- if (tis == "skin") skin_path else blood_path
      trees[[key]] <- build_centroid_tree(path, p, min_sc_size = 10)
    }
  }
  qs_save(trees, cache_trees, nthreads = 8)
}
```

For each patient, **leiden subclones** with ≥10 cells are summarised by their CNV centroid (mean signal across all genes). Subclones are then hierarchically clustered (Ward.D2, Euclidean) to give a per-patient phylogeny. Leaf label and tip size: `subclone_id (n cells | mTCM/mTRM/mCyto fractions)`.

```{r trees-plot, results='asis', fig.width=11, fig.height=8}
suppressPackageStartupMessages({library(ape); library(ggtree)})

plot_one_tree <- function(key) {
  t_obj <- trees[[key]]
  if (is.null(t_obj)) { cat("_(skipped, <2 subclones with >=10 cells)_\n\n"); return(invisible(NULL)) }
  hc <- t_obj$hc
  phy <- ape::as.phylo(hc)
  sizes <- t_obj$sizes
  bcs   <- t_obj$barcodes_per_sc
  names(bcs) <- names(sizes)
  # for each subclone, fraction of mTCM/mTRM/mCyto
  comp <- sapply(names(sizes), function(sid) {
    bc <- bcs[[sid]]
    m  <- mal_meta[match(bc, mal_meta$barcode), ]
    if (sum(!is.na(m$Malignant_T)) == 0) return(c(mTCM = 0, mTRM = 0, mCyto = 0, frac_matched = 0))
    t <- table(factor(m$Malignant_T, levels = c("mTCM", "mTRM", "mCyto")))
    frac <- as.numeric(t) / max(1, sum(t))
    c(mTCM = frac[1], mTRM = frac[2], mCyto = frac[3],
      frac_matched = sum(!is.na(m$Malignant_T)) / length(bc))
  })
  comp <- t(comp); rownames(comp) <- names(sizes)

  # build a label
  lab_df <- data.frame(
    label = names(sizes),
    n     = unname(sizes),
    pct_mTCM = round(100 * comp[, "mTCM"]),
    pct_mTRM = round(100 * comp[, "mTRM"]),
    pct_mCyto = round(100 * comp[, "mCyto"]),
    pretty = sprintf("%s (n=%d | %d/%d/%d)",
                     names(sizes), unname(sizes),
                     round(100 * comp[, "mTCM"]),
                     round(100 * comp[, "mTRM"]),
                     round(100 * comp[, "mCyto"]))
  )
  # assign dominant state for color
  lab_df$dominant <- apply(comp[, 1:3, drop = FALSE], 1, function(r) {
    if (sum(r) == 0) return("unmatched")
    c("mTCM", "mTRM", "mCyto")[which.max(r)]
  })

  # plot with ggtree
  tg <- ggtree(phy) %<+% lab_df
  p <- tg +
    geom_tippoint(aes(size = n, color = dominant)) +
    geom_tiplab(aes(label = pretty), align = TRUE, size = 3, offset = 0.02) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c(mal_colors, unmatched = "grey60")) +
    scale_size_continuous(range = c(2, 8)) +
    theme_tree() +
    xlim(0, max(ape::node.depth.edgelength(phy)) * 1.8) +
    ggtitle(sprintf("%s — subclone phylogeny (Ward.D2 on CNV centroids)", key)) +
    labs(caption = "Label: subclone_id (n cells | %mTCM / %mTRM / %mCyto)")
  print(p)
}

for (key in names(trees)) {
  parts <- strsplit(key, "::")[[1]]
  cat(sprintf("\n### %s — %s\n\n", parts[1], parts[2]))
  plot_one_tree(key)
  cat("\n\n")
}
```

## UPhyloplot2 SVG Trees {.tabset}

Subclonal phylogenies in the style of **UPhyloplot2** (Kurtenbach et al., BMC Genomics 2021) — thick-branched trees where branch width scales with subclone size, leaves labelled by letter (A–H), trunks annotated with the shared CNV events. **One tree per patient**, panels for blood patients and skin patients delivered as two combined SVGs.

**Per-patient scope (paper labels):** each patient's tree pools ALL their malignant cells across the inferCNV input subclusters — so for skin patients this includes cells originally in `Malignant T1/T2/T3`, plus `Malignant exhausted` and `Malignant Treg` (both >97% mTRM in the current `Malignant_T` annotation).

**Subclone coloring (post-processing):** UPhyloplot2 doesn't natively know about Malignant_T. After the SVG is generated, each subclone rectangle is recoloured by **the dominant Malignant_T state of the cells assigned to that subclone** (joining cell_groupings → `mal_meta`): **mTCM = darkgreen, mTRM = darkred, mCyto = darkblue**. The letter labels (A, B, C, …) stay the same; the legend on the right shows each subclone's `path → %cells → [dominant_state]`.

**State-shift markers:** for each subclone whose dominant Malignant_T state differs from its parent subclone (e.g. mTCM parent → mTRM child, indicating malignant-state differentiation within a CNV clone), a small filled **diamond** is overlaid next to the letter in the colour of the NEW state. A "Transitions:" block at the bottom of each panel's legend column lists every parent→child shift explicitly (e.g. `mTRM → mTCM at G`).

| | Patients included (≥20 malignant cells in inferCNV) | Patients skipped (too few cells) |
|---|---|---|
| **Blood** | blood 1, 2, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8 (n=734–6,452, subsampled to ≤300/tree) | — |
| **Skin**  | skin 1, 2, 3, 7, 8 (n=90–453, kept in full, subsampled to ≤300) | skin 4 (13), 5 (32), 6 (12), 9 (4) |

Each per-patient Newick is Ward.D2 hclust on Euclidean distances over the denoised CNV signal (`@expr.data`) of the cells. UPhyloplot2 (`cut_length=4`) defines subclones from the dendrogram, same as the 01b report. Trunk CNV annotations are computed from the mean CNV signal across each patient's cells (gain ≥1.05, del ≤0.95, ≥50% of arm's genes; top-10 events per patient).

```{r uphylo-setup, include=FALSE}
suppressPackageStartupMessages({library(ape); library(base64enc)})

work_root   <- "/srv/GT/analysis/p28409-Andrea/2026-03-24-UPhyloplot2-CNV-Trees"
uphylo_main <- file.path(work_root, "UPhyloplot2")
uphylo_skin  <- file.path(work_root, "uphylo_NKref/skin")
uphylo_blood <- file.path(work_root, "uphylo_NKref/blood")

setup_uphylo_dir <- function(d) {
  dir.create(file.path(d, "Inputs"), showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)
  for (f in c("uphyloplot2.py", "newick_input.py", "__init__.py", "setup.py")) {
    target <- file.path(d, f)
    src <- file.path(uphylo_main, f)
    if (!file.exists(target) && file.exists(src)) file.symlink(src, target)
  }
}
setup_uphylo_dir(uphylo_skin)
setup_uphylo_dir(uphylo_blood)

# Per-patient lists (paper labels: blood 1-8, skin 1-9; skip patients with < ~20 cells)
patients_blood <- c("blood 1", "blood 2", "blood 4", "blood 5",
                    "blood 6", "blood 7", "blood 8")
patients_skin  <- c("skin 1",  "skin 2",  "skin 3",  "skin 7", "skin 8")

# Output name: replace spaces with underscores so file paths are safe
clean_name <- function(g) gsub("\\s+", "_", g)

skin_inf_path  <- "/srv/GT/analysis/p28409-Andrea/InferCNV_published_NK_ref/skin/run.final.infercnv_obj"
blood_inf_path <- "/srv/GT/analysis/p28409-Andrea/InferCNV_published_NK_ref/blood/run.final.infercnv_obj"
```

```{r uphylo-prep-newick, include=FALSE}
build_newick_for_patient <- function(inf_obj, patient_id, work_dir, mal_meta_df,
                                     max_cells = 300, seed = 42) {
  out_name <- clean_name(patient_id)
  newick_path <- file.path(work_dir, "Inputs", paste0(out_name, ".observations_dendrogram.txt"))
  if (file.exists(newick_path)) {
    return(list(out_name = out_name, path = newick_path,
                n_cells = NA_integer_, cached = TRUE))
  }
  # All malignant cells for this patient (across all inferCNV groups), restricted
  # to those actually present in the inferCNV obs matrix.
  pat_bcs   <- mal_meta_df$barcode[mal_meta_df$patient == patient_id]
  inf_cells <- intersect(pat_bcs, colnames(inf_obj@expr.data))
  if (length(inf_cells) < 10) return(NULL)
  if (length(inf_cells) > max_cells) {
    set.seed(seed); inf_cells <- sample(inf_cells, max_cells)
  }
  mat <- inf_obj@expr.data[, inf_cells, drop = FALSE]
  d   <- dist(t(mat), method = "euclidean")
  hc  <- hclust(d, method = "ward.D2")
  phy <- ape::as.phylo(hc)
  ape::write.tree(phy, newick_path)
  list(out_name = out_name, path = newick_path, n_cells = ncol(mat), cached = FALSE)
}

needs_skin  <- !all(file.exists(file.path(uphylo_skin,  "Inputs",
                                          paste0(clean_name(patients_skin),  ".observations_dendrogram.txt"))))
needs_blood <- !all(file.exists(file.path(uphylo_blood, "Inputs",
                                          paste0(clean_name(patients_blood), ".observations_dendrogram.txt"))))

# NB. Variable names retain "_groups_info" / "groups_order" for downstream
# compatibility, but each entry is now a per-PATIENT panel, not a per-cell-type group.
skin_groups_info <- list()
if (needs_skin) {
  cat("Loading skin inferCNV object...\n")
  o_skin <- readRDS(skin_inf_path)
  skin_groups_info <- lapply(patients_skin, build_newick_for_patient,
                             inf_obj = o_skin, work_dir = uphylo_skin,
                             mal_meta_df = mal_meta)
  rm(o_skin); gc(verbose = FALSE)
} else {
  skin_groups_info <- lapply(patients_skin, function(p) {
    list(out_name = clean_name(p),
         path = file.path(uphylo_skin, "Inputs", paste0(clean_name(p), ".observations_dendrogram.txt")),
         cached = TRUE)
  })
}
names(skin_groups_info) <- patients_skin
skin_groups_info <- skin_groups_info[!sapply(skin_groups_info, is.null)]

blood_groups_info <- list()
if (needs_blood) {
  cat("Loading blood inferCNV object...\n")
  o_blood <- readRDS(blood_inf_path)
  blood_groups_info <- lapply(patients_blood, build_newick_for_patient,
                              inf_obj = o_blood, work_dir = uphylo_blood,
                              mal_meta_df = mal_meta)
  rm(o_blood); gc(verbose = FALSE)
} else {
  blood_groups_info <- lapply(patients_blood, function(p) {
    list(out_name = clean_name(p),
         path = file.path(uphylo_blood, "Inputs", paste0(clean_name(p), ".observations_dendrogram.txt")),
         cached = TRUE)
  })
}
names(blood_groups_info) <- patients_blood
blood_groups_info <- blood_groups_info[!sapply(blood_groups_info, is.null)]
```

```{r uphylo-run, include=FALSE}
conda_prefix <- "/usr/local/ngseq/miniforge3/bin/conda"

run_uphylo_for_tissue <- function(work_dir, groups_info) {
  output_svg <- file.path(work_dir, "output.svg")
  inputs_dir <- file.path(work_dir, "Inputs")

  # 1. Per-group: newick_input.py → Inputs/<out_name>.cell_groupings
  for (info in groups_info) {
    cg_file <- file.path(inputs_dir, paste0(info$out_name, ".cell_groupings"))
    if (file.exists(cg_file)) next
    if (!file.exists(info$path)) next
    cmd <- sprintf(
      'cd %s && eval "$(%s shell.bash hook)" && conda activate ps_pgueguen && printf "%%s\\n4\\n%s\\n" "%s" | python newick_input.py',
      shQuote(work_dir), conda_prefix, info$out_name, info$path
    )
    cat("newick_input.py →", info$out_name, "\n")
    system(cmd, intern = FALSE)
  }

  # 2. Run uphyloplot2.py once for the whole Inputs/ dir
  if (!file.exists(output_svg)) {
    cmd_run <- sprintf(
      'cd %s && eval "$(%s shell.bash hook)" && conda activate ps_pgueguen && python uphyloplot2.py -c 5',
      shQuote(work_dir), conda_prefix
    )
    cat("uphyloplot2.py →", work_dir, "\n")
    system(cmd_run, intern = FALSE)
  }
}

run_uphylo_for_tissue(uphylo_skin,  skin_groups_info)
run_uphylo_for_tissue(uphylo_blood, blood_groups_info)

skin_svg_raw  <- file.path(uphylo_skin,  "output.svg")
blood_svg_raw <- file.path(uphylo_blood, "output.svg")
stopifnot(file.exists(skin_svg_raw))
stopifnot(file.exists(blood_svg_raw))
```

```{r uphylo-helpers, include=FALSE}
# Recover full barcodes from a generated Newick file. newick_input.py parses our
# `AAACCTGAGACAGAGA-1_1_1` barcodes by stripping the first character AND truncating
# at the first `-`, so the cell_groupings row reads `AAACCTGAGACAGAGA` → `ACCTGAGACAGAGA`
# (the `-1_1_1` suffix also appears as a noise row like `1_1_1` we filter out).
recover_full_barcodes <- function(newick_file, cg_file) {
  tree_text <- readLines(newick_file)
  full_bcs  <- regmatches(tree_text, gregexpr("[ACGT]{10,}-[0-9]+(_[0-9]+)*", tree_text))[[1]]
  full_bcs  <- unique(full_bcs)
  # newick_input.py form = substring(2, position-of-first-dash - 1)
  trunc_form <- substring(full_bcs, 2, regexpr("-", full_bcs) - 1)
  bc_lookup  <- setNames(full_bcs, trunc_form)
  cg <- read.delim(cg_file, header = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  colnames(cg) <- c("subclone_path", "cell_barcode")
  # drop suffix-noise rows (digit/underscore-only strings, no DNA letters)
  cg <- cg[grepl("^[ACGT]+$", cg$cell_barcode), ]
  cg$full_barcode <- bc_lookup[cg$cell_barcode]
  cg
}

# UPhyloplot2 letter palette (same order as in uphyloplot2.py -c 5)
uphylo_colors  <- c("#C15A5B", "#9DABC5", "#81BED0", "#AAD1B6", "#6AAB73",
                    "#E7E689", "#D89560", "#85593E", "#C55E7B", "#9F8272",
                    "#365584", "#6582A2", "#6B8675", "#61497B", "#E7C665")
uphylo_letters <- LETTERS[1:15]

# Compute per-(patient, letter) Malignant_T composition by joining each subclone's
# cells (via cell_groupings + recovered full barcodes) to mal_meta. Returns a long
# data.frame with one row per (group, letter) and a dominant-state column.
compute_subclone_state_composition <- function(work_dir, groups_info, mal_meta_df) {
  cnv_files_dir <- file.path(work_dir, "CNV_files")
  inputs_dir    <- file.path(work_dir, "Inputs")
  rows <- list()
  for (info in groups_info) {
    cg_file  <- file.path(inputs_dir,    paste0(info$out_name, ".cell_groupings"))
    csv_file <- file.path(cnv_files_dir, paste0(info$out_name, ".cell_groupings.csv"))
    if (!file.exists(cg_file) || !file.exists(csv_file)) next
    cg <- recover_full_barcodes(info$path, cg_file)
    legend_df <- tryCatch(read.csv(csv_file, header = FALSE, fill = TRUE,
                                   stringsAsFactors = FALSE,
                                   col.names = paste0("V", 1:8)),
                          error = function(e) NULL)
    if (is.null(legend_df) || nrow(legend_df) == 0) next
    legend_df <- legend_df[grepl("^[0-9.]+$", legend_df$V1), , drop = FALSE]
    legend_df$V2 <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(legend_df$V2))
    legend_df <- legend_df[!is.na(legend_df$V2), , drop = FALSE]
    if (nrow(legend_df) == 0) next
    colnames(legend_df)[1:3] <- c("path", "pct", "letter")
    cg$path <- sub("^all_observations\\.all_observations\\.", "", cg$subclone_path)
    cg_join <- merge(cg, legend_df[, c("path", "letter", "pct")], by = "path", all.x = TRUE)
    cg_join$letter[is.na(cg_join$letter)] <- "minor"
    cg_join <- merge(cg_join,
                     mal_meta_df[, c("barcode", "Malignant_T")],
                     by.x = "full_barcode", by.y = "barcode", all.x = TRUE)
    by_letter <- cg_join |>
      dplyr::group_by(letter) |>
      dplyr::summarise(
        n_cells = dplyr::n(),
        n_matched = sum(!is.na(Malignant_T)),
        pct_mTCM  = round(100 * sum(Malignant_T == "mTCM",  na.rm = TRUE) / max(1, sum(!is.na(Malignant_T))), 1),
        pct_mTRM  = round(100 * sum(Malignant_T == "mTRM",  na.rm = TRUE) / max(1, sum(!is.na(Malignant_T))), 1),
        pct_mCyto = round(100 * sum(Malignant_T == "mCyto", na.rm = TRUE) / max(1, sum(!is.na(Malignant_T))), 1),
        .groups = "drop"
      )
    # Dominant state per letter (NA when no matched cells)
    by_letter$dominant <- ifelse(
      by_letter$n_matched == 0, NA_character_,
      c("mTCM", "mTRM", "mCyto")[apply(
        as.matrix(by_letter[, c("pct_mTCM", "pct_mTRM", "pct_mCyto")]), 1, which.max)]
    )
    by_letter$group <- info$out_name
    rows[[info$out_name]] <- by_letter[, c("group", "letter", "n_cells", "n_matched",
                                            "pct_mTCM", "pct_mTRM", "pct_mCyto", "dominant")]
  }
  do.call(rbind, rows)
}

# State colors (paper palette)
state_pal_solid <- c(mTCM = "darkgreen", mTRM = "darkred", mCyto = "darkblue")

# GRCh38 centromere positions (Mb) for p/q arm classification
chr_centro <- c(
  chr1 = 125.0, chr2 = 93.9, chr3 = 91.0, chr4 = 50.4,  chr5 = 48.8,  chr6 = 61.0,
  chr7 = 59.9,  chr8 = 45.6, chr9 = 43.0, chr10 = 39.8, chr11 = 53.4, chr12 = 35.8,
  chr13 = 17.7, chr14 = 17.2, chr15 = 19.0, chr16 = 36.6, chr17 = 25.1, chr18 = 18.5,
  chr19 = 26.5, chr20 = 28.1, chr21 = 12.0, chr22 = 15.0
) * 1e6
state_label <- c("1" = "del", "2" = "LOH", "3" = "neutral", "4" = "gain", "5" = "amp")

# Compute arm-level gain/del events from a denoised CNV signal matrix.
# inferCNV's denoised @expr.data is heavily smoothed (typical sd ~0.02), so the
# per-gene 5%-deviation thresholds (gain>1.05 / del<0.95) catch almost no genes
# even when an arm is clearly aberrant at the population level (Pair 2 truncal
# had 0 events under that scheme despite chr17q-gain / chr10p-gain visible at
# pop level). Switching to **arm-mean** thresholds matched to the actual signal
# range: gain = arm mean > 1.015, del = arm mean < 0.985 (~1× sd from baseline).
arm_events <- function(cnv_mat, cells, go, centro,
                       gain_thresh = 1.015, loss_thresh = 0.985,
                       min_genes = 5) {
  if (length(cells) == 0) return(character())
  cells <- intersect(cells, colnames(cnv_mat))
  if (length(cells) == 0) return(character())
  mean_cnv <- rowMeans(cnv_mat[, cells, drop = FALSE], na.rm = TRUE)
  m <- go[match(names(mean_cnv), rownames(go)), ]
  keep <- !is.na(m$chr)
  mean_cnv <- mean_cnv[keep]; m <- m[keep, ]
  end_col <- if ("stop" %in% colnames(m)) "stop" else "end"
  arm <- ifelse(!is.na(centro[m$chr]) & (m$start + m[[end_col]])/2 < centro[m$chr], "p", "q")
  chr_arm <- paste0(m$chr, arm)
  by_arm <- split(mean_cnv, chr_arm)
  events <- c()
  for (a in names(by_arm)) {
    if (length(by_arm[[a]]) < min_genes) next
    arm_mean <- mean(by_arm[[a]])
    if (arm_mean > gain_thresh) {
      events <- c(events, paste0(sub("^chr", "", a), " gain"))
    } else if (arm_mean < loss_thresh) {
      events <- c(events, paste0(sub("^chr", "", a), " del"))
    }
  }
  sort(events)
}

# Compute per-patient trunk events directly from the inferCNV obj.
# Replaces the old pred_cnv_regions.dat lookup (which keyed by Malignant T<N>
# groups that no longer correspond to our per-patient panel structure).
compute_patient_trunk_events <- function(inf_obj_path, patients, mal_meta_df,
                                         max_events = 10) {
  out <- setNames(vector("list", length(patients)), patients)
  if (!file.exists(inf_obj_path)) return(out)
  o <- readRDS(inf_obj_path)
  go <- as.data.frame(o@gene_order)
  for (p in patients) {
    pat_bcs <- mal_meta_df$barcode[mal_meta_df$patient == p]
    cells   <- intersect(pat_bcs, colnames(o@expr.data))
    if (length(cells) < 10) next
    events  <- arm_events(o@expr.data, cells, go, chr_centro)
    if (length(events) > max_events) events <- events[seq_len(max_events)]
    out[[p]] <- events
  }
  rm(o); gc(verbose = FALSE)
  out
}
```

```{r uphylo-enhance, include=FALSE}
enhance_svg <- function(raw_svg, work_dir, groups_info,
                        cnv_per_group = NULL,
                        groups_order = NULL, out_svg = NULL) {
  if (is.null(out_svg)) out_svg <- sub("\\.svg$", "_enhanced.svg", raw_svg)
  if (file.exists(out_svg)) return(out_svg)
  if (is.null(groups_order)) groups_order <- names(groups_info)

  svg_lines <- readLines(raw_svg)
  vb_line   <- grep("viewBox", svg_lines, value = TRUE)[1]
  vb_nums   <- as.numeric(regmatches(vb_line, gregexpr("[0-9]+\\.?[0-9]*", vb_line))[[1]])
  svg_w     <- vb_nums[3]; svg_h <- vb_nums[4]

  # --- Compute per-(patient, letter) dominant Malignant_T state ------------
  # Used to recolour the subclone rects from UPhyloplot2's default 15-color palette
  # to paper-canonical state colors (mTCM=darkgreen / mTRM=darkred / mCyto=darkblue).
  comp_df <- tryCatch(
    compute_subclone_state_composition(work_dir, groups_info, mal_meta),
    error = function(e) NULL)
  dominant_lookup <- list()
  if (!is.null(comp_df) && nrow(comp_df) > 0) {
    for (g in unique(comp_df$group)) {
      sub <- comp_df[comp_df$group == g, ]
      m <- setNames(rep(NA_character_, length(uphylo_letters)), uphylo_letters)
      for (i in seq_len(nrow(sub))) {
        if (sub$letter[i] %in% uphylo_letters) m[sub$letter[i]] <- sub$dominant[i]
      }
      dominant_lookup[[g]] <- m
    }
  }

  # Per-group legend from CNV_files/<out_name>.cell_groupings.csv
  cnv_files_dir <- file.path(work_dir, "CNV_files")
  csv_files <- list.files(cnv_files_dir, pattern = "\\.csv$", full.names = TRUE)
  legend_entries <- list()
  # uphyloplot2.py writes ragged CSVs (some rows have 5+ cols when there are many subclones).
  # read.csv then over-splits some rows into spurious single-letter "rows" with NA in V2.
  read_uphylo_csv <- function(f) {
    df <- tryCatch(read.csv(f, header = FALSE, fill = TRUE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE,
                            col.names = paste0("V", 1:8)),
                   error = function(e) NULL)
    if (is.null(df) || nrow(df) == 0) return(NULL)
    df <- df[grepl("^[0-9.]+$", df$V1), , drop = FALSE]     # keep numeric paths
    df$V2 <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(df$V2))
    df <- df[!is.na(df$V2), , drop = FALSE]
    df
  }
  for (csv_file in csv_files) {
    pt_name <- sub("\\.cell_groupings\\.csv$", "", basename(csv_file))
    df <- read_uphylo_csv(csv_file)
    if (is.null(df) || nrow(df) == 0) next
    df <- df[df$V2 > 0, , drop = FALSE]
    for (i in seq_len(nrow(df))) {
      lbl <- trimws(df$V3[i])
      if (!is.na(lbl) && nchar(lbl) > 0) {
        legend_entries[[paste0(pt_name, "_", lbl)]] <- list(
          patient = pt_name, label = lbl,
          subclone = df$V1[i], pct = round(df$V2[i], 1)
        )
      }
    }
  }
  pt_names <- intersect(sapply(groups_info, `[[`, "out_name"), unique(sapply(legend_entries, `[[`, "patient")))

  legend_x_start <- svg_w + 10
  legend_y_start <- 40
  row_h <- 18
  col_w <- 130
  legend_svg <- character()
  for (pi in seq_along(pt_names)) {
    pt <- pt_names[pi]
    col_x <- legend_x_start + (pi - 1) * col_w
    legend_svg <- c(legend_svg, sprintf(
      '<text x="%s" y="%s" font-family="ArialMT" font-size="10" font-weight="bold">%s</text>',
      col_x, legend_y_start - 5, pt))
    pt_entries <- Filter(function(e) e$patient == pt, legend_entries)
    for (ei in seq_along(pt_entries)) {
      e <- pt_entries[[ei]]
      y_pos   <- legend_y_start + (ei - 1) * row_h + 10
      dom     <- dominant_lookup[[pt]][e$label]
      col     <- if (!is.na(dom)) state_pal_solid[[dom]] else "#888888"
      dom_lbl <- if (!is.na(dom)) dom else "?"
      legend_svg <- c(legend_svg,
        sprintf('<rect x="%s" y="%s" width="12" height="12" rx="2" fill="%s"/>',
                col_x, y_pos - 10, col),
        sprintf('<text x="%s" y="%s" font-family="ArialMT" font-size="9">%s  %s (%.1f%%) [%s]</text>',
                col_x + 16, y_pos, e$label, e$subclone, e$pct, dom_lbl))
    }
  }

  # Trunk CNV annotations per panel — UPhyloplot2 spaces panels by 110 px starting at x=60
  # `cnv_per_group` is now a per-PATIENT named list (computed by compute_patient_trunk_events).
  trunk_annot_svg <- character()
  if (!is.null(cnv_per_group)) {
    for (pi in seq_along(groups_order)) {
      g <- groups_order[pi]
      annots <- cnv_per_group[[g]]
      if (length(annots) == 0) next
      trunk_x <- 60 + (pi - 1) * 110
      losses <- annots[grepl("LOH|del", annots)]
      gains  <- annots[grepl("gain|amp", annots)]
      for (li in seq_along(losses)) {
        trunk_annot_svg <- c(trunk_annot_svg, sprintf(
          '<text x="%s" y="%s" font-family="ArialMT" font-size="7" text-anchor="end" font-style="italic">%s</text>',
          trunk_x - 12, 50 + (li - 1) * 10, losses[li]))
      }
      for (gi in seq_along(gains)) {
        trunk_annot_svg <- c(trunk_annot_svg, sprintf(
          '<text x="%s" y="%s" font-family="ArialMT" font-size="7" font-style="italic">%s</text>',
          trunk_x + 12, 50 + (gi - 1) * 10, gains[gi]))
      }
    }
  }

  # --- Recolor subclone rects by dominant Malignant_T state ---------------
  # UPhyloplot2 outputs its own 15-color palette per subclone letter (A=#C15A5B, ...).
  # We replace each <rect>'s fill with the paper-canonical color of that subclone's
  # dominant state. Panel index is inferred from rect x: panel pi is centered at
  # x = 60 + (pi-1)*110 (UPhyloplot2 convention with `--space_between_plots=110`).
  letter_for_color <- setNames(uphylo_letters, tolower(uphylo_colors))
  # Fallback per-patient overall dominant state (used for subclones too small to
  # appear in UPhyloplot2's legend; otherwise their rects would stay in the
  # default palette).
  # Key the lookup by BOTH the space-form ("skin 7" — as in groups_order) AND
  # the underscore-form ("skin_7" — as used by csv basenames / out_names) so it
  # works whether the caller uses the panel-order name or the file-stem name.
  patient_overall_dom <- c()
  for (g in groups_order) {
    pat_id <- if (grepl(" ", g)) g else gsub("_", " ", g)
    pat_states <- mal_meta$Malignant_T[mal_meta$patient == pat_id]
    pat_states <- pat_states[!is.na(pat_states)]
    if (length(pat_states) > 0) {
      tbl <- table(pat_states)
      dom <- names(tbl)[which.max(tbl)]
      patient_overall_dom[g] <- dom
      patient_overall_dom[gsub(" ", "_", g)] <- dom
    }
  }
  for (idx in seq_along(svg_lines)) {
    line <- svg_lines[idx]
    if (!grepl("<rect", line)) next
    m_x <- regmatches(line, regexpr('x="[0-9.]+"', line))
    m_fill <- regmatches(line, regexpr('fill="#[0-9A-Fa-f]{6}"', line))
    if (length(m_x) == 0 || length(m_fill) == 0) next
    x_val <- as.numeric(sub('x="', '', sub('"$', '', m_x)))
    fill_val_raw <- sub('fill="', '', sub('"$', '', m_fill))
    fill_val <- tolower(fill_val_raw)
    letter <- letter_for_color[fill_val]
    if (is.na(letter)) next  # not a UPhyloplot2 palette color
    panel_idx <- floor(x_val / 110) + 1
    if (panel_idx < 1 || panel_idx > length(groups_order)) next
    # dominant_lookup is keyed by out_name (underscore form), groups_order has
    # the space form — normalise to underscore so per-subclone state takes effect.
    panel_pat <- gsub(" ", "_", groups_order[panel_idx])
    dom <- dominant_lookup[[panel_pat]][letter]
    # Fall back to overall patient state for unlabeled (minor) subclones
    if (is.null(dom) || is.na(dom)) dom <- patient_overall_dom[panel_pat]
    if (is.na(dom)) next
    new_fill <- state_pal_solid[[dom]]
    new_line <- sub(m_fill, sprintf('fill="%s"', new_fill), line, fixed = TRUE)
    svg_lines[idx] <- new_line
  }

  # --- State-shift markers ---------------------------------------------------
  # For each legend subclone, infer its parent subclone (path "1.2.1" → "1.2"),
  # compare the dominant Malignant_T state of parent vs child. If different, mark
  # that letter on the tree with a small colored chevron arrow + record the shift
  # for a per-panel "Transitions" list in the side legend.
  path_to_letter_by_pt <- list()
  for (e in legend_entries) {
    pt <- e$patient
    if (is.null(path_to_letter_by_pt[[pt]])) path_to_letter_by_pt[[pt]] <- character()
    path_to_letter_by_pt[[pt]][e$subclone] <- e$label
  }
  # Walk up the subclone path tree to find the nearest LABELED ancestor (a path
  # that has a letter assigned in the legend). UPhyloplot2 omits letters for
  # subclones <5% of cells, so a direct parent_path may not be in the legend —
  # in that case we keep walking up. If no labeled ancestor is found, fall back
  # to the patient's overall dominant state.
  find_labeled_parent <- function(path, lookup) {
    p <- path
    while (TRUE) {
      new_p <- sub("\\.[^.]+$", "", p)
      if (new_p == p) return(NA_character_)  # no dot left → walked past root
      p <- new_p
      val <- lookup[p]
      if (!is.null(val) && !is.na(val)) return(unname(val))
    }
  }
  transitions <- list()
  for (e in legend_entries) {
    pt   <- e$patient
    path <- e$subclone
    if (path == "1" || !grepl("\\.", path)) next  # root has no parent
    parent_letter <- find_labeled_parent(path, path_to_letter_by_pt[[pt]])
    this_dom <- dominant_lookup[[pt]][e$label]
    if (is.na(this_dom)) next
    parent_dom <- if (!is.na(parent_letter)) dominant_lookup[[pt]][parent_letter] else NA
    if (is.na(parent_dom)) parent_dom <- patient_overall_dom[pt]  # fallback
    if (is.na(parent_dom) || this_dom == parent_dom) next
    transitions[[length(transitions) + 1]] <- list(
      patient = pt, letter = e$label,
      parent_letter = if (is.na(parent_letter)) "<patient>" else parent_letter,
      from = parent_dom, to = this_dom)
  }
  # Find letter tspan positions in SVG and overlay a colored marker on transitions
  shift_marker_svg <- character()
  if (length(transitions) > 0) {
    trans_key <- vapply(transitions, function(t) paste0(t$patient, "_", t$letter), character(1))
    for (line in svg_lines) {
      m <- regmatches(line, regexec(
        '<tspan x="([0-9.]+) *" y="([0-9.]+)"[^>]*>([A-N])</tspan>', line))[[1]]
      if (length(m) < 4) next
      x_val <- as.numeric(m[2]); y_val <- as.numeric(m[3]); letter <- m[4]
      panel_idx <- floor(x_val / 110) + 1
      if (panel_idx < 1 || panel_idx > length(groups_order)) next
      panel_pat <- gsub(" ", "_", groups_order[panel_idx])  # match transitions' underscore-form keys
      key <- paste0(panel_pat, "_", letter)
      i <- match(key, trans_key)
      if (is.na(i)) next
      tr <- transitions[[i]]
      to_color <- state_pal_solid[[tr$to]]
      # small filled diamond next to the letter, in the NEW state's color
      shift_marker_svg <- c(shift_marker_svg, sprintf(
        '<polygon points="%.2f,%.2f %.2f,%.2f %.2f,%.2f %.2f,%.2f" fill="%s" stroke="white" stroke-width="0.6"/>',
        x_val + 6, y_val - 4,        # top
        x_val + 9, y_val - 1,        # right
        x_val + 6, y_val + 2,        # bottom
        x_val + 3, y_val - 1,        # left
        to_color))
    }
  }

  # Append a "Transitions:" sub-section to each panel's legend column
  transitions_legend_svg <- character()
  if (length(transitions) > 0) {
    by_pt <- split(transitions, vapply(transitions, `[[`, character(1), "patient"))
    legend_x_start_local <- svg_w + 10
    for (pi in seq_along(pt_names)) {
      pt <- pt_names[pi]
      if (is.null(by_pt[[pt]])) next
      col_x <- legend_x_start_local + (pi - 1) * col_w
      pt_entries_local <- Filter(function(e) e$patient == pt, legend_entries)
      base_y <- legend_y_start + length(pt_entries_local) * row_h + 18
      transitions_legend_svg <- c(transitions_legend_svg, sprintf(
        '<text x="%s" y="%s" font-family="ArialMT" font-size="9" font-weight="bold">Transitions:</text>',
        col_x, base_y))
      for (j in seq_along(by_pt[[pt]])) {
        tr <- by_pt[[pt]][[j]]
        y_pos <- base_y + j * 12
        transitions_legend_svg <- c(transitions_legend_svg, sprintf(
          '<text x="%s" y="%s" font-family="ArialMT" font-size="8">%s → %s at %s</text>',
          col_x, y_pos, tr$from, tr$to, tr$letter))
      }
    }
  }

  new_w <- svg_w + length(pt_names) * col_w + 20
  legend_max_rows <- if (length(pt_names) == 0) 0 else max(sapply(pt_names, function(pt)
    sum(sapply(legend_entries, function(e) e$patient == pt))))
  # Also reserve vertical space for trunk annotations
  trunk_max_y <- 0
  if (length(trunk_annot_svg) > 0) {
    ys_matches <- regmatches(trunk_annot_svg, regexpr('y="[0-9]+"', trunk_annot_svg))
    ys <- as.numeric(gsub('[y="]', "", ys_matches))
    if (length(ys) > 0) trunk_max_y <- max(ys, na.rm = TRUE)
  }
  new_h <- max(svg_h,
               legend_y_start + legend_max_rows * row_h + 30,
               trunk_max_y + 20)
  svg_lines[1] <- sprintf('<svg viewBox="0 0 %s %s" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">', new_w, new_h)

  # Bump canvas height to fit the transitions block in the legend column
  trans_max_rows <- if (length(transitions) == 0) 0 else
    max(sapply(pt_names, function(pt) sum(vapply(transitions, function(t) t$patient == pt, logical(1))))) + 2
  new_h <- max(new_h, legend_y_start + (legend_max_rows + trans_max_rows) * row_h + 30)
  svg_lines[1] <- sprintf('<svg viewBox="0 0 %s %s" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">', new_w, new_h)

  close_idx <- grep("</svg>", svg_lines)
  svg_lines <- c(svg_lines[1:(close_idx - 1)],
                 "", "<!-- Trunk CNV annotations -->", trunk_annot_svg,
                 "", "<!-- Per-group legend -->", legend_svg,
                 "", "<!-- State-shift markers on tree -->", shift_marker_svg,
                 "", "<!-- Transition list in legend -->", transitions_legend_svg,
                 svg_lines[close_idx:length(svg_lines)])
  writeLines(svg_lines, out_svg)
  out_svg
}

# Per-patient trunk events computed directly from CNV signal (cached for re-renders).
cache_trunk_skin  <- file.path(work_root, "cache_01c_trunk_events_skin.qs2")
cache_trunk_blood <- file.path(work_root, "cache_01c_trunk_events_blood.qs2")
if (file.exists(cache_trunk_skin)) {
  skin_trunk_events <- qs_read(cache_trunk_skin,  nthreads = 4)
} else {
  skin_trunk_events  <- compute_patient_trunk_events(skin_inf_path,  patients_skin,  mal_meta)
  qs_save(skin_trunk_events,  cache_trunk_skin,  nthreads = 4)
}
if (file.exists(cache_trunk_blood)) {
  blood_trunk_events <- qs_read(cache_trunk_blood, nthreads = 4)
} else {
  blood_trunk_events <- compute_patient_trunk_events(blood_inf_path, patients_blood, mal_meta)
  qs_save(blood_trunk_events, cache_trunk_blood, nthreads = 4)
}

skin_svg  <- enhance_svg(skin_svg_raw,  uphylo_skin,  skin_groups_info,
                         cnv_per_group = skin_trunk_events,
                         groups_order = names(skin_groups_info))
blood_svg <- enhance_svg(blood_svg_raw, uphylo_blood, blood_groups_info,
                         cnv_per_group = blood_trunk_events,
                         groups_order = names(blood_groups_info))
```

### Skin

```{r uphylo-display-skin, results='asis'}
svg_raw <- readLines(skin_svg) |> paste(collapse = "\n")
b64 <- base64enc::base64encode(charToRaw(svg_raw))
cat(sprintf('<img src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,%s" style="width:100%%; max-width:1400px;" />\n', b64))
```

### Blood

```{r uphylo-display-blood, results='asis'}
svg_raw <- readLines(blood_svg) |> paste(collapse = "\n")
b64 <- base64enc::base64encode(charToRaw(svg_raw))
cat(sprintf('<img src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,%s" style="width:100%%; max-width:1400px;" />\n', b64))
```

### Subclone composition

For each UPhyloplot2 subclone letter, count how many cells are mTCM / mTRM / mCyto by joining recovered barcodes into `CTCL_blood_skin_malignant.qs2`. This is the headline biological readout — mixed-state subclones confirm that the NK-reference fix surfaces mTCM clones that 01b missed.

```{r uphylo-comp-table, results='asis'}
build_composition <- function(work_dir, groups_info, tissue) {
  cnv_files_dir <- file.path(work_dir, "CNV_files")
  inputs_dir    <- file.path(work_dir, "Inputs")
  rows <- list()
  for (info in groups_info) {
    cg_file     <- file.path(inputs_dir,    paste0(info$out_name, ".cell_groupings"))
    csv_file    <- file.path(cnv_files_dir, paste0(info$out_name, ".cell_groupings.csv"))
    newick_file <- info$path
    if (!file.exists(cg_file) || !file.exists(csv_file)) next
    cg <- recover_full_barcodes(newick_file, cg_file)
    legend_df <- tryCatch(read.csv(csv_file, header = FALSE, fill = TRUE,
                                   stringsAsFactors = FALSE,
                                   col.names = paste0("V", 1:8)),
                          error = function(e) NULL)
    if (is.null(legend_df) || nrow(legend_df) == 0) next
    # Same ragged-CSV cleanup as enhance_svg()
    legend_df <- legend_df[grepl("^[0-9.]+$", legend_df$V1), , drop = FALSE]
    legend_df$V2 <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(legend_df$V2))
    legend_df <- legend_df[!is.na(legend_df$V2), , drop = FALSE]
    if (nrow(legend_df) == 0) next
    colnames(legend_df)[1:3] <- c("path", "pct", "letter")
    # cell_groupings uses "all_observations.all_observations.<path>", CSV uses "<path>"
    cg$path <- sub("^all_observations\\.all_observations\\.", "", cg$subclone_path)
    cg_join <- merge(cg, legend_df[, c("path", "letter", "pct")], by = "path", all.x = TRUE)
    cg_join$letter[is.na(cg_join$letter)] <- "minor"
    # join to Seurat meta by full_barcode
    cg_join <- merge(cg_join, mal_meta, by.x = "full_barcode", by.y = "barcode", all.x = TRUE)
    by_letter <- cg_join |>
      dplyr::group_by(letter) |>
      dplyr::summarise(
        n_cells = dplyr::n(),
        n_matched = sum(!is.na(Malignant_T)),
        pct_mTCM  = round(100 * sum(Malignant_T == "mTCM",  na.rm = TRUE) / max(1, sum(!is.na(Malignant_T))), 1),
        pct_mTRM  = round(100 * sum(Malignant_T == "mTRM",  na.rm = TRUE) / max(1, sum(!is.na(Malignant_T))), 1),
        pct_mCyto = round(100 * sum(Malignant_T == "mCyto", na.rm = TRUE) / max(1, sum(!is.na(Malignant_T))), 1),
        patients  = paste(sort(unique(stats::na.omit(patient))), collapse = ", "),
        .groups   = "drop"
      ) |>
      dplyr::mutate(tissue = tissue, group = info$out_name) |>
      dplyr::select(tissue, group, letter, n_cells, n_matched, pct_mTCM, pct_mTRM, pct_mCyto, patients)
    rows[[info$out_name]] <- by_letter
  }
  do.call(rbind, rows)
}

comp_skin  <- build_composition(uphylo_skin,  skin_groups_info,  "skin")
comp_blood <- build_composition(uphylo_blood, blood_groups_info, "blood")
comp_all   <- rbind(comp_skin, comp_blood)

# Headline finding 1: subclones with internal mTCM/mTRM coexistence (≥10% of each, n≥10)
mixed_within <- comp_all |>
  dplyr::filter(letter != "minor",
                pct_mTCM >= 10, pct_mTRM >= 10,
                n_matched >= 10)

# Headline finding 2: GROUPS containing BOTH mTCM-dominant AND mTRM-dominant subclones
# (a stronger phylogenetic signature: same patient's malignancy carries clones in different states)
group_summary <- comp_all |>
  dplyr::filter(letter != "minor", n_matched >= 5) |>
  dplyr::group_by(tissue, group) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    has_mTCM_dom = any(pct_mTCM >= 60),
    has_mTRM_dom = any(pct_mTRM >= 60),
    has_mCyto_dom = any(pct_mCyto >= 50),
    n_subclones = dplyr::n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
mixed_across <- group_summary |> dplyr::filter(has_mTCM_dom & has_mTRM_dom)

cat(sprintf("**Headline 1 (within-subclone coexistence):** %d subclones (letter labels) contain ≥10%% mTCM AND ≥10%% mTRM cells — direct evidence of state interconversion within a clone.\n\n",
            nrow(mixed_within)))
cat(sprintf("**Headline 2 (across-subclone coexistence within patient):** %d of %d patient groups carry BOTH an mTCM-dominant (≥60%% mTCM) subclone AND an mTRM-dominant (≥60%% mTRM) subclone — proves both states are sustained by the same malignant clonal pool, not separate lineages.\n\n",
            nrow(mixed_across), nrow(group_summary)))
if (nrow(mixed_across) > 0) {
  cat("Groups with coexisting mTCM-dominant and mTRM-dominant subclones:\n\n")
  mixed_across |>
    dplyr::select(tissue, group, n_subclones) |>
    knitr::kable(format = "markdown") |> cat(sep = "\n")
  cat("\n\n")
}
knitr::kable(comp_all, caption = "UPhyloplot2 subclone composition (mTCM / mTRM / mCyto per letter, %)")
```

## Cell-Level NJ Fan Trees {.tabset}

For each malignant patient group, an unrooted neighbour-joining tree built on per-cell CNV signal (≤300 cells per group, same Newick used by UPhyloplot2), rendered in **fan layout** with tips colored by **Malignant_T state**. Visualizes which lineages within the inferred CNV phylogeny are mTCM-dominated versus mTRM-dominated — directly comparable to the equivalent panel in the 01b report.

```{r fan-trees-setup, include=FALSE}
suppressPackageStartupMessages({library(ape); library(ggtree)})

state_pal <- c("mTCM" = "darkgreen", "mTRM" = "darkred", "mCyto" = "darkblue",  # paper palette
               "unknown" = "grey80")

# Helper: build tip annotation for a Newick file by joining Seurat metadata
fan_tip_annot <- function(newick_path) {
  tr <- ape::read.tree(newick_path)
  # tips are the full barcodes we wrote (e.g. AAACCTGAGACAGAGA-1_1_1)
  m <- mal_meta[match(tr$tip.label, mal_meta$barcode), ]
  tip_df <- data.frame(
    label = tr$tip.label,
    state = ifelse(is.na(m$Malignant_T), "unknown", as.character(m$Malignant_T)),
    tissue = ifelse(is.na(m$tissue), "unknown", as.character(m$tissue)),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  list(tree = tr, tip_df = tip_df)
}

# Per-patient panels (uses uphylo-prep-newick output paths). Names are the patient
# ids themselves (e.g. "blood 1", "skin 2"), already tissue-prefixed.
fan_tabs <- c(
  setNames(lapply(skin_groups_info,  function(i) list(tissue = "skin",  info = i)),
           names(skin_groups_info)),
  setNames(lapply(blood_groups_info, function(i) list(tissue = "blood", info = i)),
           names(blood_groups_info))
)
```

```{r fan-trees-plot, results='asis', fig.width=10, fig.height=7}
for (tab in names(fan_tabs)) {
  spec <- fan_tabs[[tab]]
  cat(sprintf("\n### %s\n\n", tab))
  newick_path <- spec$info$path
  if (!file.exists(newick_path)) { cat("_(no cached Newick)_\n\n"); next }
  ta <- fan_tip_annot(newick_path)
  if (length(ta$tree$tip.label) < 5) { cat("_(too few cells for a tree)_\n\n"); next }
  p <- ggtree::ggtree(ta$tree, layout = "fan") %<+% ta$tip_df +
    ggtree::geom_tippoint(aes(color = state), size = 1.5, alpha = 0.9) +
    scale_color_manual(values = state_pal, name = "state") +
    ggtitle(sprintf("%s — NJ fan tree (n=%d cells) — by Malignant_T",
                    tab, length(ta$tree$tip.label))) +
    theme(legend.position = "right")
  print(p)
  cat("\n\n")
}
```

## NJ Fan Trees by Patient {.tabset}

Same cached Newicks as the previous section, but tips coloured by `patient` instead of Malignant_T state. Useful as a sanity check: for the **quasi-per-patient** blood groups (`Malignant T1`-`T8`) the trees should be (near-)monochromatic, confirming the per-patient interpretation. For the **multi-patient** groups (`Malignant T shared`, `Malignant exhausted`, `Malignant Treg`) the tree is multi-coloured — showing whether cells from the same patient cluster together (i.e. form a sub-branch) or are scattered across the topology.

```{r fan-trees-by-patient, results='asis', fig.width=10, fig.height=7}
# Build a patient palette spanning ALL patients in the malignant Seurat object
all_patients <- sort(unique(stats::na.omit(mal_meta$patient)))
patient_pal  <- setNames(unname(pals::polychrome())[seq_along(all_patients) + 2],
                         all_patients)
patient_pal["unknown"] <- "grey80"

fan_tip_annot_patient <- function(newick_path) {
  tr <- ape::read.tree(newick_path)
  m  <- mal_meta[match(tr$tip.label, mal_meta$barcode), ]
  tip_df <- data.frame(
    label = tr$tip.label,
    patient = ifelse(is.na(m$patient), "unknown", as.character(m$patient)),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  list(tree = tr, tip_df = tip_df)
}

for (tab in names(fan_tabs)) {
  spec <- fan_tabs[[tab]]
  cat(sprintf("\n### %s\n\n", tab))
  newick_path <- spec$info$path
  if (!file.exists(newick_path)) { cat("_(no cached Newick)_\n\n"); next }
  ta <- fan_tip_annot_patient(newick_path)
  if (length(ta$tree$tip.label) < 5) { cat("_(too few cells for a tree)_\n\n"); next }
  n_pat <- length(setdiff(unique(ta$tip_df$patient), "unknown"))
  cat(sprintf("_n=%d cells, %d distinct patient(s) in this group_\n\n",
              length(ta$tree$tip.label), n_pat))
  p <- ggtree::ggtree(ta$tree, layout = "fan") %<+% ta$tip_df +
    ggtree::geom_tippoint(aes(color = patient), size = 1.5, alpha = 0.9) +
    scale_color_manual(values = patient_pal, name = "patient") +
    ggtitle(sprintf("%s — NJ fan tree by patient", tab)) +
    theme(legend.position = "right")
  print(p)
  cat("\n\n")
}
```

## Clonal Tracking (TCR) {.tabset}

TCR clonotypes uniquely identify each malignant lineage independent of CNV. Of the 20,946 malignant cells, **12,879 (61%) carry TCR data** (scRepertoire `CTstrict`). Across the cohort there are **112 unique CTstrict clones**, dominated by a handful of hyperexpanded clones. The two paired-compartment patients (Pair 1: BL1+SK2; Pair 2: BL4+SK3) each carry **one dominant shared clone** spanning blood and skin AND containing both mTCM and mTRM cells — direct phylogenetic evidence (independent of CNV) that the two memory states belong to the same malignant clone.

```{r tcr-setup, include=FALSE}
suppressPackageStartupMessages({library(dplyr); library(tidyr); library(ggplot2); library(patchwork)})

# Build per-cell long table with clone identity
mal_tcr <- mal_obj@meta.data |>
  dplyr::mutate(barcode = colnames(mal_obj),
                CTstrict = as.character(CTstrict),
                CTaa = as.character(CTaa)) |>
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(CTstrict)) |>
  dplyr::select(barcode, CTstrict, CTaa, cloneSize, patient, tissue, Malignant_T)

# Compute per-clone summary (sorted by total size)
clone_tbl <- mal_tcr |>
  dplyr::group_by(CTstrict) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    n_total  = dplyr::n(),
    n_blood  = sum(tissue == "blood"),
    n_skin   = sum(tissue == "skin"),
    n_mTCM   = sum(Malignant_T == "mTCM"),
    n_mTRM   = sum(Malignant_T == "mTRM"),
    n_mCyto  = sum(Malignant_T == "mCyto"),
    patient  = paste(sort(unique(patient)), collapse = ", "),
    n_patients = dplyr::n_distinct(patient),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(n_total)) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    clone_id = sprintf("clone_%d", dplyr::row_number()),
    pct_mTCM = round(100 * n_mTCM / n_total, 1),
    pct_mTRM = round(100 * n_mTRM / n_total, 1),
    pct_mCyto = round(100 * n_mCyto / n_total, 1),
    # Tissue-spanning: ≥1 cell in BOTH compartments (skin biopsies are inherently sparse;
    # even 1 cell from the same TCR clone in skin is biologically meaningful).
    spans_tissues = (n_blood >= 1) & (n_skin >= 1),
    # State-spanning: ≥5 mTCM AND ≥5 mTRM cells (absolute counts — mTRM is rare in
    # blood-heavy patients, so percentage thresholds wash out the signal).
    spans_states  = (n_mTCM >= 5) & (n_mTRM >= 5)
  )

# Join clone_id back onto cell-level table for downstream plotting
clone_id_lookup <- setNames(clone_tbl$clone_id, clone_tbl$CTstrict)
mal_tcr$clone_id <- clone_id_lookup[mal_tcr$CTstrict]

# Top-N expanded clones for figure focus
top_n_clones <- 10
top_clones    <- clone_tbl |> dplyr::slice_head(n = top_n_clones)
top_clone_ids <- top_clones$clone_id

# Palette for top clones
top_palette <- setNames(unname(pals::polychrome())[seq_len(top_n_clones) + 2], top_clone_ids)
```

### Setup overview

**Coverage**: `r format(nrow(mal_tcr), big.mark = ",")` cells with TCR / `r format(ncol(mal_obj), big.mark = ",")` malignant cells (`r round(100*nrow(mal_tcr)/ncol(mal_obj))`%). **Unique CTstrict clones**: `r nrow(clone_tbl)`. **Top `r top_n_clones` expanded clones cover** `r round(100*sum(top_clones$n_total)/nrow(mal_tcr))`% **of TCR-typed cells**.

```{r tcr-setup-table}
top_clones |>
  dplyr::select(clone_id, n_total, n_blood, n_skin,
                pct_mTCM, pct_mTRM, pct_mCyto, patient,
                spans_tissues, spans_states) |>
  knitr::kable(caption = sprintf("Top %d expanded TCR clones (CTstrict)", top_n_clones))
```

### Shared clones (paired patients)

```{r tcr-paired-bars, fig.width=14, fig.height=6}
paired_clone_panel <- function(pair_label, blood_id, skin_id) {
  cells <- mal_tcr |> dplyr::filter(patient %in% c(blood_id, skin_id))
  if (nrow(cells) == 0) return(NULL)
  shared_clones <- cells |>
    dplyr::group_by(clone_id) |>
    dplyr::summarise(n_blood = sum(tissue == "blood"),
                     n_skin  = sum(tissue == "skin"),
                     n_total = dplyr::n(),
                     .groups = "drop") |>
    dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(n_total)) |>
    dplyr::slice_head(n = 10)
  plot_df <- cells |>
    dplyr::filter(clone_id %in% shared_clones$clone_id) |>
    dplyr::count(clone_id, tissue, Malignant_T) |>
    dplyr::mutate(clone_id = factor(clone_id, levels = shared_clones$clone_id))
  ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = clone_id, y = n, fill = Malignant_T)) +
    geom_col() +
    facet_wrap(~ tissue, ncol = 1, scales = "free_y") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = state_pal) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
    labs(x = NULL, y = "n cells", title = pair_label,
         subtitle = sprintf("blood patient: %s • skin patient: %s", blood_id, skin_id))
}

p1 <- paired_clone_panel("Pair 1 (BL1 + SK2)", "blood 1", "skin 2")
p2 <- paired_clone_panel("Pair 2 (BL4 + SK3)", "blood 4", "skin 3")
if (!is.null(p1) && !is.null(p2)) print(p1 | p2) else if (!is.null(p1)) print(p1) else if (!is.null(p2)) print(p2)
```

### Top clones × tissue × state (headline) {.active}

```{r tcr-tissue-state-heatmap, fig.width=11, fig.height=8}
suppressPackageStartupMessages({library(ComplexHeatmap); library(circlize)})

cell_counts <- mal_tcr |>
  dplyr::filter(clone_id %in% clone_tbl$clone_id[seq_len(20)]) |>
  dplyr::mutate(group = paste(tissue, Malignant_T, sep = "/")) |>
  dplyr::count(clone_id, group) |>
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = group, values_from = n, values_fill = 0)
# Ensure all 6 group columns present
target_groups <- c("blood/mTCM", "blood/mTRM", "blood/mCyto",
                   "skin/mTCM",  "skin/mTRM",  "skin/mCyto")
for (g in setdiff(target_groups, colnames(cell_counts))) cell_counts[[g]] <- 0
cell_counts <- cell_counts |> dplyr::select(clone_id, dplyr::all_of(target_groups))

# Order by total size (matches clone_tbl ranking)
cell_counts <- cell_counts[match(clone_tbl$clone_id[seq_len(20)], cell_counts$clone_id), ]
cell_counts <- cell_counts[!is.na(cell_counts$clone_id), ]

mat <- as.matrix(cell_counts[, -1])
rownames(mat) <- cell_counts$clone_id
mat_log <- log10(mat + 1)

clone_info <- clone_tbl[match(rownames(mat), clone_tbl$clone_id), ]
row_ann <- rowAnnotation(
  n_total         = anno_barplot(clone_info$n_total, gp = gpar(fill = "grey50")),
  spans_tissues   = clone_info$spans_tissues,
  spans_states    = clone_info$spans_states,
  patient_main    = clone_info$patient,
  col = list(
    spans_tissues = c("TRUE" = "#1B7837", "FALSE" = "grey90"),
    spans_states  = c("TRUE" = "#762A83", "FALSE" = "grey90")
  ),
  annotation_name_side = "top",
  simple_anno_size = unit(4, "mm")
)
col_split <- factor(sub("/.*", "", colnames(mat)), levels = c("blood", "skin"))

ht <- Heatmap(
  mat_log,
  name = "log10(n+1)",
  col  = colorRamp2(c(0, 1, 3), c("white", "#FCBBA1", "#67000D")),
  cluster_rows = FALSE, cluster_columns = FALSE,
  show_row_names = TRUE, row_names_side = "left",
  column_split = col_split,
  column_title_gp = gpar(fontsize = 11, fontface = "bold"),
  column_names_rot = 35,
  cell_fun = function(j, i, x, y, w, h, fill) {
    if (mat[i, j] > 0) grid.text(mat[i, j], x, y, gp = gpar(fontsize = 8))
  },
  right_annotation = row_ann,
  border = TRUE,
  column_title = "Top 20 TCR clones × tissue × Malignant_T state (cell counts)"
)
draw(ht, heatmap_legend_side = "left", annotation_legend_side = "left")
```

### Alluvial: clone → tissue → state

```{r tcr-alluvial, fig.width=12, fig.height=7}
if (requireNamespace("ggalluvial", quietly = TRUE)) {
  library(ggalluvial)
  alluv_df <- mal_tcr |>
    dplyr::filter(clone_id %in% top_clone_ids) |>
    dplyr::count(clone_id, tissue, Malignant_T) |>
    dplyr::mutate(clone_id = factor(clone_id, levels = top_clone_ids),
                  tissue = factor(tissue, levels = c("blood", "skin")),
                  Malignant_T = factor(Malignant_T, levels = c("mTCM", "mTRM", "mCyto")))
  p <- ggplot(alluv_df,
              aes(y = n, axis1 = clone_id, axis2 = tissue, axis3 = Malignant_T)) +
    geom_alluvium(aes(fill = clone_id), width = 1/8) +
    geom_stratum(width = 1/8, fill = "grey85", color = "white") +
    geom_text(stat = "stratum", aes(label = after_stat(stratum)), size = 3) +
    scale_x_discrete(limits = c("Clone", "Tissue", "State"), expand = c(.1, .1)) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = top_palette) +
    theme_minimal() +
    labs(y = "n cells", title = sprintf("Top %d clones: clone → tissue → state", top_n_clones))
  print(p)
} else {
  cat("_ggalluvial not installed — alluvial plot omitted_\n")
}
```

### UMAP overlay

```{r tcr-umap, fig.width=14, fig.height=7}
umap_df <- as.data.frame(mal_obj@reductions$umap@cell.embeddings)
colnames(umap_df) <- c("UMAP_1", "UMAP_2")
umap_df$barcode <- rownames(umap_df)
umap_df <- umap_df |>
  dplyr::left_join(mal_tcr |> dplyr::select(barcode, clone_id, tissue), by = "barcode") |>
  dplyr::mutate(clone_plot = ifelse(clone_id %in% top_clone_ids, clone_id, NA_character_))

plot_umap_clones <- function(df, title) {
  bg <- df[is.na(df$clone_plot), , drop = FALSE]
  fg <- df[!is.na(df$clone_plot), , drop = FALSE]
  ggplot() +
    geom_point(data = bg, aes(UMAP_1, UMAP_2), color = "grey85", size = 0.3) +
    geom_point(data = fg, aes(UMAP_1, UMAP_2, color = clone_plot), size = 0.6) +
    scale_color_manual(values = top_palette, name = "clone") +
    theme_bw() + theme(aspect.ratio = 1) + ggtitle(title)
}
p_all   <- plot_umap_clones(umap_df, sprintf("All cells — top %d clones overlaid", top_n_clones))
p_blood <- plot_umap_clones(umap_df |> dplyr::filter(tissue == "blood" | is.na(tissue)), "Blood cells")
p_skin  <- plot_umap_clones(umap_df |> dplyr::filter(tissue == "skin"  | is.na(tissue)), "Skin cells")
print(p_all | (p_blood / p_skin))
```

### Per-clone state composition

```{r tcr-state-comp, fig.width=12, fig.height=7}
comp_df <- mal_tcr |>
  dplyr::filter(clone_id %in% top_clone_ids) |>
  dplyr::count(clone_id, tissue, Malignant_T) |>
  dplyr::mutate(clone_id = factor(clone_id, levels = top_clone_ids))
ggplot(comp_df, aes(x = tissue, y = n, fill = Malignant_T)) +
  geom_col() +
  facet_wrap(~ clone_id, scales = "free_y", ncol = 5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = state_pal) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(y = "n cells",
       title = sprintf("State composition per tissue, top %d clones", top_n_clones))
```

### Clonal overlap (Morisita)

```{r tcr-overlap, fig.width=8, fig.height=6}
# Morisita-Horn between (tissue × state) groups, across all patients
mh_index <- function(a, b) {
  # a, b: named numeric vectors of clone counts
  all_clones <- union(names(a), names(b))
  a <- a[all_clones]; a[is.na(a)] <- 0
  b <- b[all_clones]; b[is.na(b)] <- 0
  if (sum(a) == 0 || sum(b) == 0) return(NA_real_)
  pa <- a / sum(a); pb <- b / sum(b)
  num <- 2 * sum(pa * pb)
  den <- sum(pa^2) + sum(pb^2)
  if (den == 0) NA_real_ else num / den
}
groups_df <- mal_tcr |> dplyr::mutate(group = paste(tissue, Malignant_T, sep = "/"))
group_clone_counts <- split(groups_df, groups_df$group) |> lapply(function(d) table(d$clone_id))

grp_names <- names(group_clone_counts)
M <- matrix(NA_real_, length(grp_names), length(grp_names),
            dimnames = list(grp_names, grp_names))
for (i in seq_along(grp_names)) for (j in seq_along(grp_names)) {
  M[i, j] <- mh_index(group_clone_counts[[i]], group_clone_counts[[j]])
}
Heatmap(M, name = "Morisita",
        col = colorRamp2(c(0, 0.5, 1), c("white", "#FDAE61", "#A50026")),
        cluster_rows = FALSE, cluster_columns = FALSE,
        cell_fun = function(j, i, x, y, w, h, fill) {
          if (!is.na(M[i, j])) grid.text(sprintf("%.2f", M[i, j]), x, y, gp = gpar(fontsize = 9))
        },
        column_title = "Clonal overlap between (tissue × state) groups (cohort-pooled)")
```

### Summary table

```{r tcr-summary-table}
clone_tbl |>
  dplyr::slice_head(n = 20) |>
  dplyr::select(clone_id, n_total, n_blood, n_skin,
                pct_mTCM, pct_mTRM, pct_mCyto,
                patient, n_patients, spans_tissues, spans_states) |>
  knitr::kable(caption = "Top 20 TCR clones — span flags identify the headline clones")
```

### NJ fan trees coloured by TCR clone

```{r tcr-fan-trees, results='asis', fig.width=10, fig.height=7}
suppressPackageStartupMessages({library(ape); library(ggtree)})
clone_bc_lookup <- setNames(mal_tcr$clone_id, mal_tcr$barcode)

for (tab in names(fan_tabs)) {
  spec <- fan_tabs[[tab]]
  cat(sprintf("\n#### %s\n\n", tab))
  newick_path <- spec$info$path
  if (!file.exists(newick_path)) { cat("_(no cached Newick)_\n\n"); next }
  tr <- ape::read.tree(newick_path)
  tip_df <- data.frame(
    label = tr$tip.label,
    clone_id = clone_bc_lookup[tr$tip.label],
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  tip_df$clone_plot <- ifelse(tip_df$clone_id %in% top_clone_ids, tip_df$clone_id, "other/none")
  pal_local <- c(top_palette, "other/none" = "grey85")
  p <- ggtree(tr, layout = "fan") %<+% tip_df +
    geom_tippoint(aes(color = clone_plot), size = 1.5, alpha = 0.9) +
    scale_color_manual(values = pal_local, name = "clone") +
    ggtitle(sprintf("%s — NJ fan tree by TCR clone", tab)) +
    theme(legend.position = "right")
  print(p)
  cat("\n\n")
}
```

## Joint Blood + Skin Trees per Pair {.tabset}

**Only two patients in the cohort have BOTH blood and skin samples** — Pair 1 (BL1 + SK2) and Pair 2 (BL4 + SK3) — so blood↔skin clonal sharing can only be evaluated in these two individuals. The other 13 patients are single-tissue. This section pools the paired blood and skin malignant cells into **one** phylogenetic tree per pair, with two coloured views: (i) by tissue × Malignant_T state, (ii) by **per-pair sharing status** of the TCR clones. The second panel is the direct visualization of clonal sharing — every shared clone gets its own colour, and shared-clone cells should appear on BOTH the blood- and skin-derived branches of the tree.

**Why this is feasible:** both inferCNV runs used **NK cells as reference** with the same `denoise=TRUE` settings, so the denoised CNV signal is comparable across runs (mean blood = 1.002 / sd = 0.018; mean skin = 1.001 / sd = 0.026; same ~1.0 baseline). The gene sets overlap on **3,540 common genes** (95% of skin's 4,096; 63% of blood's 5,582) — more than enough for a stable Ward.D2 distance.

**Cells per pair (in inferCNV obs):** Pair 1 = 2,811 blood + 109 skin; Pair 2 = 2,855 blood + 132 skin. Blood is subsampled to ≤300 per pair (set.seed=42) so the tree stays readable; all skin cells are kept.

```{r joint-pair-setup, include=FALSE}
suppressPackageStartupMessages({library(ape); library(ggtree); library(patchwork)})

pairs_def <- list(
  list(label = "Pair 1 (BL1 + SK2)", blood_pat = "blood 1", skin_pat = "skin 2"),
  list(label = "Pair 2 (BL4 + SK3)", blood_pat = "blood 4", skin_pat = "skin 3")
)

cache_joint <- "/srv/GT/analysis/p28409-Andrea/2026-03-24-UPhyloplot2-CNV-Trees/cache_01c_joint_pair_trees.qs2"
build_joint_tree <- function(blood_pat, skin_pat, max_blood_cells = 300, seed = 42) {
  o_blood <- readRDS("/srv/GT/analysis/p28409-Andrea/InferCNV_published_NK_ref/blood/run.final.infercnv_obj")
  o_skin  <- readRDS("/srv/GT/analysis/p28409-Andrea/InferCNV_published_NK_ref/skin/run.final.infercnv_obj")

  bl_bcs_all <- mal_meta$barcode[mal_meta$patient == blood_pat]
  sk_bcs_all <- mal_meta$barcode[mal_meta$patient == skin_pat]
  bl_bcs <- intersect(bl_bcs_all, colnames(o_blood@expr.data))
  sk_bcs <- intersect(sk_bcs_all, colnames(o_skin@expr.data))
  if (length(bl_bcs) > max_blood_cells) {
    set.seed(seed); bl_bcs <- sample(bl_bcs, max_blood_cells)
  }

  common_genes <- intersect(rownames(o_blood@expr.data), rownames(o_skin@expr.data))
  m_bl <- o_blood@expr.data[common_genes, bl_bcs, drop = FALSE]
  m_sk <- o_skin@expr.data[common_genes, sk_bcs, drop = FALSE]
  mat  <- cbind(m_bl, m_sk)
  rm(o_blood, o_skin); gc(verbose = FALSE)

  list(matrix = mat, blood_bcs = bl_bcs, skin_bcs = sk_bcs)
}

if (file.exists(cache_joint)) {
  joint_data <- qs_read(cache_joint, nthreads = 8)
} else {
  joint_data <- lapply(pairs_def, function(p)
    build_joint_tree(p$blood_pat, p$skin_pat))
  names(joint_data) <- sapply(pairs_def, `[[`, "label")
  qs_save(joint_data, cache_joint, nthreads = 8)
}
```

```{r joint-pair-trees, results='asis', fig.width=14, fig.height=8}
# Paper palette: mTCM=darkgreen, mTRM=darkred, mCyto=darkblue. Lighter variants for blood,
# canonical paper colors for skin (skin is the focal tissue in CTCL studies).
state_pal_joint <- c("blood_mTCM" = "#5C9F5C", "blood_mTRM" = "#C75C5C", "blood_mCyto" = "#5C5CC7",
                     "skin_mTCM"  = "darkgreen", "skin_mTRM"  = "darkred", "skin_mCyto" = "darkblue",
                     "unknown" = "grey80")

# Per-pair sharing requires the per-cell TCR table. Build a cell → CTstrict lookup once.
ctstrict_by_bc <- setNames(as.character(mal_obj$CTstrict), colnames(mal_obj))

for (pair in pairs_def) {
  d <- joint_data[[pair$label]]
  cat(sprintf("\n### %s\n\n", pair$label))
```
