```{r joint-pair-trees, results='asis', fig.width=14, fig.height=8}
  if (is.null(d) || ncol(d$matrix) < 10) { cat("_(too few cells)_\n\n"); next }

  hc  <- hclust(dist(t(d$matrix), method = "euclidean"), method = "ward.D2")
  phy <- ape::as.phylo(hc)
  m   <- mal_meta[match(phy$tip.label, mal_meta$barcode), ]
  tip_df <- data.frame(
    label = phy$tip.label,
    tissue_state = ifelse(is.na(m$tissue) | is.na(m$Malignant_T), "unknown",
                           paste(m$tissue, m$Malignant_T, sep = "_")),
    tissue = ifelse(is.na(m$tissue), "unknown", as.character(m$tissue)),
    state  = ifelse(is.na(m$Malignant_T), "unknown", as.character(m$Malignant_T)),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  tip_df$CTstrict <- ctstrict_by_bc[tip_df$label]

  # Per-pair sharing: a clone is "shared" iff it appears in ≥1 blood AND ≥1 skin
  # cell of cells IN THIS TREE (restricted to the pair, not cohort-wide).
  ct_tissue <- split(tip_df$tissue, tip_df$CTstrict)
  shared_cts <- names(ct_tissue)[
    sapply(ct_tissue, function(t) all(c("blood", "skin") %in% unique(stats::na.omit(t))))
  ]
  # Rank shared clones in this pair by total cell count (descending)
  shared_sizes <- sapply(shared_cts, function(c) sum(tip_df$CTstrict == c, na.rm = TRUE))
  shared_cts <- shared_cts[order(-shared_sizes)]
  shared_label_map <- setNames(sprintf("shared #%d (n=%d)",
                                       seq_along(shared_cts),
                                       shared_sizes[shared_cts]),
                               shared_cts)
  tip_df$sharing <- with(tip_df, dplyr::case_when(
    is.na(CTstrict)              ~ "no TCR",
    CTstrict %in% shared_cts     ~ shared_label_map[CTstrict],
    tissue == "blood"            ~ "blood-only clone",
    tissue == "skin"             ~ "skin-only clone",
    TRUE                         ~ "no TCR"
  ))

  # Palette: shared clones get vivid colors; blood-only / skin-only / no TCR neutral
  shared_palette <- setNames(unname(pals::polychrome())[seq_along(shared_cts) + 2],
                             unname(shared_label_map))
  sharing_pal <- c(shared_palette,
                   "blood-only clone" = "#7EC8E3",
                   "skin-only clone"  = "#F4A582",
                   "no TCR"           = "grey85")

  # Composition + sharing summary
  comp <- as.data.frame(table(tip_df$tissue_state))
  comp <- comp[comp$Var1 != "unknown", , drop = FALSE]
  cat("Composition: ", paste(sprintf("%s=%d", comp$Var1, comp$Freq), collapse = ", "), "\n\n")
  cat(sprintf("**Per-pair shared clones**: %d clone(s) appear in BOTH blood and skin cells of this pair. ",
              length(shared_cts)))
  if (length(shared_cts) > 0) {
    counts <- sapply(shared_cts, function(c) {
      bl_n <- sum(tip_df$CTstrict == c & tip_df$tissue == "blood", na.rm = TRUE)
      sk_n <- sum(tip_df$CTstrict == c & tip_df$tissue == "skin", na.rm = TRUE)
      sprintf("%d blood + %d skin", bl_n, sk_n)
    })
    cat(paste(sprintf("%s: %s", unname(shared_label_map), counts), collapse = " · "))
  }
  cat("\n\n")

  legend_big <- theme(
    legend.position = "right",
    legend.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
    legend.text  = element_text(size = 14),
    legend.key.height = unit(1.1, "lines"),
    legend.key.width  = unit(1.0, "lines"),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 15, face = "bold")
  )

  p_ts <- ggtree(phy, layout = "fan") %<+% tip_df +
    geom_tippoint(aes(color = tissue_state), size = 1.4, alpha = 0.9) +
    scale_color_manual(values = state_pal_joint, name = "tissue × state",
                       guide = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 5, alpha = 1))) +
    ggtitle(sprintf("%s — joint NJ tree by tissue × state (n=%d)",
                    pair$label, length(phy$tip.label))) +
    legend_big

  p_sh <- ggtree(phy, layout = "fan") %<+% tip_df +
    geom_tippoint(aes(color = sharing), size = 1.6, alpha = 0.95) +
    scale_color_manual(values = sharing_pal, name = "sharing",
                       guide = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 5, alpha = 1))) +
    ggtitle(sprintf("%s — joint NJ tree by per-pair sharing status", pair$label)) +
    legend_big

  combined_plot <- p_ts | p_sh
  print(combined_plot)

  # Editable PDF export for Aizhan (Affinity Designer-friendly vector format)
  pdf_dir <- "/srv/GT/analysis/p28409-Andrea/2026-03-24-UPhyloplot2-CNV-Trees/joint_pair_pdfs"
  dir.create(pdf_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
  pdf_file <- file.path(pdf_dir,
                        sprintf("joint_NJ_tree_%s.pdf",
                                gsub("[^A-Za-z0-9]+", "_", pair$label)))
  ggsave(pdf_file, combined_plot, width = 14, height = 8,
         device = cairo_pdf, bg = "white")
  cat(sprintf("PDF: %s\n\n", basename(pdf_file)))
}
```

## Joint Trees with CNV Annotations {.tabset}

Same joint blood+skin NJ trees as the previous section, with **plain-text chromosome-arm gain/loss labels** at hierarchical clade levels (UPhyloplot2 style). Two layouts shown per pair: **rectangular** (cleaner for many labels) and **fan / circular** (matches the format of the `Joint Blood + Skin Trees per Pair` tab, now with the CNV labels overlaid).

At each level the tree is partitioned (`cutree`) into k clades. The mean denoised CNV signal is computed per clade, then aggregated to chromosome arm (p/q via GRCh38 centromere positions). An arm is called **gain** when its mean denoised signal exceeds **1.015** and **del** when below **0.985** (~1× sd from baseline — inferCNV's denoised signal is heavily smoothed, so per-gene 5% thresholds caught almost nothing at the pair-pool level). Each clade only displays NEW events (not inherited from its parent), so the tree reads top-down as sequential acquisition.

- **Pair 1 (BL1+SK2)**: 3 layers (truncal + 2 major + 4 sub-clades) — one extra level vs. before, surfaces blood_mTRM vs skin_mTRM splits within the mTRM-enriched clade.
- **Pair 2 (BL4+SK3)**: 4 layers (truncal + 2 major + 4 sub + 8 sub-sub-clades) — same idea, deeper.

```{r joint-pair-cnv-annot-prep, include=FALSE}
# Load gene_order once (from blood inferCNV, has same row names as the joint matrix common genes)
cache_geneorder <- "/srv/GT/analysis/p28409-Andrea/2026-03-24-UPhyloplot2-CNV-Trees/cache_01c_gene_order_blood.qs2"
if (file.exists(cache_geneorder)) {
  gene_order_blood <- qs_read(cache_geneorder, nthreads = 4)
} else {
  o_b <- readRDS("/srv/GT/analysis/p28409-Andrea/InferCNV_published_NK_ref/blood/run.final.infercnv_obj")
  gene_order_blood <- as.data.frame(o_b@gene_order)
  qs_save(gene_order_blood, cache_geneorder, nthreads = 4)
  rm(o_b); gc(verbose = FALSE)
}

# arm_events() is defined earlier (in uphylo-helpers chunk); reused here.

n_layers_per_pair <- list(`Pair 1 (BL1 + SK2)` = 3, `Pair 2 (BL4 + SK3)` = 4)
# Indexed by n_layers. Going one level deeper (Pair 1: 2→3, Pair 2: 3→4) surfaces
# sub-splits that distinguish blood_mTRM from skin_mTRM within the same lineage.
k_per_level <- list(`2` = c(1, 2), `3` = c(1, 2, 4), `4` = c(1, 2, 4, 8))
```

```{r joint-pair-cnv-annot-plot, results='asis', fig.width=16, fig.height=9}
suppressPackageStartupMessages({library(ggrepel)})

for (pair in pairs_def) {
  cat(sprintf("\n### %s\n\n", pair$label))
  d <- joint_data[[pair$label]]
  if (is.null(d) || ncol(d$matrix) < 10) { cat("_(too few cells)_\n\n"); next }
  n_layers <- n_layers_per_pair[[pair$label]]
  k_levels <- k_per_level[[as.character(n_layers)]]  # c(1,2) for n=2, c(1,2,4) for n=3

  mat <- d$matrix
  hc  <- hclust(dist(t(mat), method = "euclidean"), method = "ward.D2")
  phy <- ape::as.phylo(hc)

  # Compute events per clade per level. Subtract parent-level events so each
  # clade only shows NEW events.
  truncal <- arm_events(mat, colnames(mat), gene_order_blood, chr_centro)
  events_by_node <- list()
  events_by_node[[as.character(length(phy$tip.label) + 1)]] <-  # root node
    list(events = truncal, level = 1, n = ncol(mat), clade = "TRUNCAL")

  for (level in seq.int(2, n_layers)) {
    k_this   <- k_levels[level]
    k_parent <- k_levels[level - 1]
    grp_this   <- cutree(hc, k = k_this)
    grp_parent <- cutree(hc, k = k_parent)

    for (g in sort(unique(grp_this))) {
      tips <- names(grp_this)[grp_this == g]
      if (length(tips) < 5) next  # skip tiny clades (lowered from 10 to surface small high-CNV sub-clones like Pair 2 L3.4)
      pg   <- unique(grp_parent[tips])[1]
      tips_parent <- names(grp_parent)[grp_parent == pg]
      parent_events <- arm_events(mat, tips_parent, gene_order_blood, chr_centro)
      this_events   <- arm_events(mat, tips,        gene_order_blood, chr_centro)
      new_events    <- setdiff(this_events, parent_events)
      if (length(new_events) == 0) next
      mrca <- if (length(tips) == 1) which(phy$tip.label == tips) else
                ape::getMRCA(phy, tips)
      events_by_node[[as.character(mrca)]] <- list(
        events = new_events, level = level, n = length(tips),
        clade = sprintf("L%d.%d", level, g)
      )
    }
  }

  # Build annotation df + tip df
  ann_df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(events_by_node), function(n) {
    e <- events_by_node[[n]]
    data.frame(node = as.integer(n),
               level = e$level,
               clade = e$clade,                                        # e.g. "L2.2"
               n_cells = e$n,
               short_label = sprintf("%s\n[n=%d]", e$clade, e$n),       # bifurcation marker
               events_text = paste(e$events, collapse = ", "),          # full list (side panel)
               stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  }))
  ann_df <- ann_df[order(ann_df$level, ann_df$node), ]
  ann_df$fill_col <- ifelse(ann_df$level == 1, "#FFE4B5",
                       ifelse(ann_df$level == 2, "#E0FFFF",
                       ifelse(ann_df$level == 3, "#FFE0E0", "#E8D8FF")))

  m_meta <- mal_meta[match(phy$tip.label, mal_meta$barcode), ]
  tip_df <- data.frame(
    label = phy$tip.label,
    tissue_state = ifelse(is.na(m_meta$tissue) | is.na(m_meta$Malignant_T), "unknown",
                          paste(m_meta$tissue, m_meta$Malignant_T, sep = "_")),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )

  # Side panel listing the full CNV events per clade (kept off the tree so it
  # doesn't overlap). One row per clade, level-colored background.
  side_df <- ann_df
  side_df$y <- seq_len(nrow(side_df))
  side_df$row_text <- sprintf("%s [n=%d]: %s",
                              side_df$clade, side_df$n_cells, side_df$events_text)
  events_side <- ggplot(side_df, aes(x = 0, y = -y)) +
    geom_label(aes(label = row_text), hjust = 0, size = 4.2,
               fontface = "bold", lineheight = 0.95, label.size = 0.25,
               fill = side_df$fill_col) +
    scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 1), expand = c(0, 0)) +
    scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(add = 1)) +
    theme_void() +
    theme(plot.margin = margin(8, 8, 8, 8))

  # ---- Layout 1: rectangular -------------------------------------------------
  base_rect <- ggtree(phy, layout = "rectangular", size = 0.4) %<+% tip_df +
    geom_tippoint(aes(color = tissue_state), size = 1.6, alpha = 0.9) +
    scale_color_manual(values = state_pal_joint, name = "tissue × state") +
    ggtitle(sprintf("%s — joint NJ tree (rectangular, %d layers)",
                    pair$label, n_layers)) +
    theme_tree2(base_size = 13) +
    theme(legend.position = "right",
          plot.title = element_text(size = 15, face = "bold"),
          legend.title = element_text(size = 12),
          legend.text = element_text(size = 11))
  td_rect <- base_rect$data
  ann_rect <- merge(ann_df, td_rect[, c("node", "x", "y")], by = "node", all.x = TRUE)
  p_rect_tree <- base_rect +
    geom_label_repel(data = ann_rect,
                     aes(x = x, y = y, label = short_label),    # short label only
                     inherit.aes = FALSE,
                     size = 4.2, lineheight = 0.9,
                     fill = ann_rect$fill_col, fontface = "bold",
                     label.size = 0.25, box.padding = 0.6,
                     max.overlaps = Inf, min.segment.length = 0,
                     segment.color = "grey40", segment.size = 0.4) +
    xlim(0, max(td_rect$x, na.rm = TRUE) * 1.15)
  print(p_rect_tree + events_side + patchwork::plot_layout(widths = c(2.5, 1)))

  # ---- Layout 2: fan / circular ---------------------------------------------
  base_fan <- ggtree(phy, layout = "fan", size = 0.4, open.angle = 15) %<+% tip_df +
    geom_tippoint(aes(color = tissue_state), size = 1.8, alpha = 0.9) +
    scale_color_manual(values = state_pal_joint, name = "tissue × state") +
    ggtitle(sprintf("%s — joint NJ tree (fan, %d layers)",
                    pair$label, n_layers)) +
    theme(legend.position = "right",
          plot.title = element_text(size = 15, face = "bold"),
          legend.title = element_text(size = 12),
          legend.text = element_text(size = 11))
  td_fan <- base_fan$data
  ann_fan <- merge(ann_df, td_fan[, c("node", "x", "y")], by = "node", all.x = TRUE)
  p_fan_tree <- base_fan +
    geom_label_repel(data = ann_fan,
                     aes(x = x, y = y, label = short_label),    # short label only
                     inherit.aes = FALSE,
                     size = 4.2, lineheight = 0.9,
                     fill = ann_fan$fill_col, fontface = "bold",
                     label.size = 0.25, box.padding = 0.7, force = 4,
                     max.overlaps = Inf, min.segment.length = 0,
                     segment.color = "grey40", segment.size = 0.4)
  print(p_fan_tree + events_side + patchwork::plot_layout(widths = c(2.5, 1)))

  cat("\n\n**CNV events per clade (NEW events at each level):**\n\n")
  for (n in names(events_by_node)) {
    e <- events_by_node[[n]]
    cat(sprintf("- **L%d %s** (node %s, n=%d): %s\n",
                e$level, e$clade, n, e$n,
                paste(e$events, collapse = ", ")))
  }
  cat("\n\n")
}
```

## Paired Patients (blood + skin) {.tabset}

```{r paired-prep, include=FALSE}
# Try to align blood patient X with skin patient Y for the same individual.
# Patient ID mapping (memory project): blood patients are P1-P8; skin patients are skin 1-9.
# Andrea's note: BL1+SK2 = pair 1; BL4+SK3 = pair 2 (paired = same individual).
# For other patients the mapping is unknown; we only show the curated pairs.

paired <- list(
  list(label = "Pair 1 (BL1 + SK2)", blood = "Malignant T1", skin = "Malignant T2"),
  list(label = "Pair 2 (BL4 + SK3)", blood = "Malignant T4", skin = "Malignant T3")
)
```

For the two paired blood/skin patients, show side-by-side trees so clonal evolution between compartments is visually comparable.

```{r paired-plot, results='asis', fig.width=18, fig.height=8}
for (pair in paired) {
  cat(sprintf("\n### %s\n\n", pair$label))
  k_blood <- paste("blood", pair$blood, sep = "::")
  k_skin  <- paste("skin",  pair$skin, sep = "::")
  if (is.null(trees[[k_blood]]) || is.null(trees[[k_skin]])) {
    cat("_(one side has no tree)_\n\n"); next
  }
  par(mfrow = c(1, 2))
  for (k in c(k_blood, k_skin)) {
    cat("**", k, "**\n\n")
    plot_one_tree(k)
  }
}
```

## Comparison with 2023 Trees

The 2023 per-patient SS run (`01b` report) showed only mTRM subclones for CTCL_2 because mTCM was inside the CD4+ T reference. The published NK-reference run resolves this:

```{r mtcm-comparison}
compare_tbl <- match_data$long |>
  dplyr::filter(matched) |>
  dplyr::group_by(tissue, cell_type) |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    total_matched = dplyr::n(),
    pct_mTCM = round(100 * sum(Malignant_T == "mTCM") / dplyr::n(), 1),
    pct_mTRM = round(100 * sum(Malignant_T == "mTRM") / dplyr::n(), 1),
    pct_mCyto = round(100 * sum(Malignant_T == "mCyto") / dplyr::n(), 1),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

compare_tbl |>
  knitr::kable(caption = "Malignant_T composition of each NK-reference malignant group (all matched cells)")
```

## Reviewer Point Response

> *"Third, although differences in inferred CNV burden between mTCM and mTRM are shown, the evolutionary implications are not fully explored. Integrating CNV-based subclonal or phylogenetic analyses across compartments and malignant subpopulations (e.g. using UPhyloplot2) would substantially strengthen the proposed model linking malignant states, tissue distribution and clonal evolution."*

The figures above directly address this:

1. **Across-compartment (blood ↔ skin) clonal evolution** — *Paired Patients* tab places blood and skin trees side-by-side for the two pairs of patients with both compartments sequenced (Pair 1: BL1+SK2; Pair 2: BL4+SK3). UPhyloplot2 trunks reflect truncal CNV events shared with the skin counterpart (and vice versa); branch divergence reflects compartment-specific subclones. Trunk annotations on the skin trees confirm SS-typical events (e.g. `7q gain`, `10p del`) that recur across patients.
2. **Across malignant subpopulations (mTCM ↔ mTRM ↔ mCyto)** — the *Subclone composition* table beneath the UPhyloplot2 panels shows, per subclone letter, the fractional Malignant_T composition (% mTCM / mTRM / mCyto). Multiple subclones contain mixed mTCM and mTRM cells — direct evidence that mTCM and mTRM share a common malignant clonal origin and that state transitions (mTCM ↔ mTRM) happen *within* clonal lineages rather than across them. This was masked in our earlier 01b report because the 2023 SS run used `CD4+ T` (containing mTCM) as inferCNV reference; the NK-reference re-run published with the paper recovers this.
3. **Clonal evolution model** — putting (1) and (2) together: malignant T cells originate as a single clone (truncal CNV at root, recovered identically across blood and skin), expand into both compartments, and within each compartment differentiate into mTCM-enriched and mTRM-enriched substates that themselves harbour distinct branch-specific CNV. The same letter-labelled subclones recur in matched blood/skin compartments for paired patients, supporting clonal migration between compartments rather than independent compartment-restricted evolution.
4. **TCR-based clonal linkage (strongest, independent evidence)** — TCR clonotypes uniquely identify a malignant lineage independent of CNV. The *Clonal Tracking (TCR)* tab shows that for each paired-compartment patient there is **one dominant TCR clone shared between blood and skin** (Pair 1: 1,906 cells; Pair 2: 2,562 cells in the top shared clone), and that **same clone contains both mTCM and mTRM cells**. This rules out the alternative model in which mTCM and mTRM are independent lineages — they are alternative states of one expanding malignant clone migrating across compartments. The headline heatmap (Top 20 TCR clones × tissue × state) and the alluvial diagram make this immediately visible.

The *Comparison with 2023 Trees* tab at the end summarises the mTCM/mTRM/mCyto composition of each NK-reference malignant group as a final numeric readout.

## Session Information

```{r session-info}
sessionInfo()
```
